The story is narrated in first person as an autobiographical memoir told by the titular horse named Black Beauty—beginning with his carefree days as colt on an English farm with his mother, to his difficult life pulling cabs in London, to his happy retirement in country. Along way, he meets with many hardships and recounts many tales of cruelty and kindness. Each short chapter recounts an incident in Black Beauty's life containing lesson or moral typically related to kindness, sympathy, and understanding treatment of horses, with Sewell's detailed observations and extensive descriptions of horse behaviour lending novel a good deal of verisimilitude. The book describes conditions among London horse-drawn taxicab drivers, including the financial hardship caused to them by high licence fees and low, legally fixed fares. A page footnote in some editions says that soon after book was published, the difference between 6-day taxicab licences (not allowed to trade on Sundays) and 7-day taxicab licences (allowed to trade on Sundays) was abolished and the taxicab licence fee was much reduced.

Despite the fact that Weir has been with Liverpool since the age of eighteen and only made his first-team debut in August of last year, he has allegedly already been approached by Jack Ross for Sunderland and is said to be very keen to move. Sunderland, who are struggling in the League One division, have not yet won a game. But they consider that Weir’s experience and technical ability would be an excellent addition to their squad. Liverpool’s manager, Jurgen Klopp, has previously stated that he does not wish to sell any of his players during the winter transfer window, but is thought to be ready to allow Weir to leave if the right offer comes in. Weir, if he were to go to Sunderland, would follow in the footsteps of his former Liverpool team-mates Steven Gerrard and Jordan Henderson, who both played at Sunderland before moving to Anfield.

Image segmentation is a popular area of research in computer vision that has
many applications in automated image processing. A recent technique called
piecewise flat embeddings (PFE) has been proposed for use in image
segmentation; PFE transforms image pixel data into a lower dimensional
representation where similar pixels are pulled close together and dissimilar
pixels are pushed apart. This technique has shown promising results, but its
original formulation is not computationally feasible for large images. We
propose two improvements to the algorithm for computing PFE: first, we
reformulate portions of the algorithm to enable various linear algebra
operations to be performed in parallel; second, we propose utilizing an
iterative linear solver (preconditioned conjugate gradient) to quickly solve a
linear least-squares problem that occurs in the inner loop of a nested
iteration. With these two computational improvements, we show on a publicly
available image database that PFE can be sped up by an order of magnitude
without sacrificing segmentation performance. Our results make this technique
more practical for use on large data sets, not only for image segmentation, but
for general data clustering problems.

Summer Bread Salad Recipe

Ingredients:
- 1 loaf of crusty bread, sliced into bite-sized pieces
- 2 cups cherry tomatoes, halved
- 1 cucumber, diced
- 1 red onion, thinly sliced
- 1 avocado, diced
- ½ cup fresh basil leaves, chopped
- ¼ cup fresh parsley leaves, chopped
- 3 tablespoons olive oil
- 2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar
- 1 clove garlic, minced
- Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions:

1. In a large bowl, combine the sliced bread, cherry tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, avocado, basil, and parsley.

2. In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, balsamic vinegar, garlic, salt, and pepper. Pour the dressing over the salad and toss until everything is evenly coated.

3. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least an hour before serving. This will allow the flavors to meld together and the vegetables to soften.

4. When you're ready to serve, give the salad another gentle toss and sprinkle with additional fresh herbs if desired. Serve chilled or at room temperature. Enjoy your delicious summer bread salad!

The plot revolves around fourteen colonists of the world Delmak-O. They are: Betty Jo Berm, a linguist; elderly Bert Kostler, settlement custodian; Maggie Walsh, a theologian; Ignatz Thugg, who oversees thermoplastics; Milton Babble, a physician; Wade Frazer, a psychologist; Tony Dunkelwelt, a geologist; Glen Belsnor, who specialises in telecommunications; Susie Smart, a typist; Roberta Rockingham, a sociologist; Ben Tallchief, a naturalist; Seth and Mary Morley, a marine biologist couple; and Ned Russell, an economist. They inhabit a universe in which the deities of their religion can be contacted through a network of prayer amplifiers and transmitters. Tallchief is transferred to Delmak-O as a direct result of his prayer-petition for a change in his intolerable work situation. Delmak-O is a mysterious and largely unexplored world. It seems to be inhabited by both real and artificial beings and enormous cube-shaped, gelatinous objects ("tenches") that duplicate items presented to them and give out advice, in anagrams reminiscent of the I Ching. In addition, various members of the group report sightings of a large "Building". As various calamities continue to befall each character, part of the group ventures out to find the mysterious structure. Each member of the group perceives the Building's entrance motto, and thus its purpose, differently. One by one, the characters Tallchief, Smart, Berm, Dunkelwelt, Kostler and Walsh either kill themselves or are killed under mysterious circumstances. During a fight between the remaining colonists Seth Morley is shot through the shoulder causing an artery to be severed. While recovering from an attempt to repair the artery, Morley is abducted by armed men who kill Belsnor. They put Morley aboard a small flying craft but Morley overpowers them and takes control of the craft. With it he discovers that Delmak-O is in fact Earth, and he returns to the group to report this. The group then comes to the conclusion that they are all criminally insane and part of a psychiatric experiment in rehabilitation. Once they admit to having killed the other members they conclude that the experiment has been a failure. It is at this point that they notice that each of them is tattooed with the phrase, "Persus 9". They decide to ask a tench what this means but doing so causes the tench to explode and the world around them to crumble to pieces. All of them, including the colonists thought to be dead, awake to find that they are actually the crew of a spaceship that has become stranded in orbit around a dead star with no way of calling for help. Their experiance had been a kind of virtual reality—a computer-generated religion that synthesized their beliefs—to help them pass the time. Seth Morley is depressed by this and wonders wether it would be better to let all the air out from the ship and thus kill them all rather than live out the rest of their lives engaging in virtual reality with no hope of rescue. Before he can act, however, an aspect of the deity known as the Intercessor, supposedly existing only in the virtual reality program and not a part of the "real" world, appears before Morley and removes him from the ship. The others, unconcerned with his disappearance, embark on another hallucination which begins in exactly the same way as the previous one, only this time without Seth Morley.

8 small fennel-heads, with fronds removed and kept for the garnish, 4 tablespoons of olive oil, 2 oranges, peeled, and cut into rings, 1 red onion, peeled, halved, and sliced, 2 tablespoons of fresh orange juice, salt, ground black pepper, 2 tablespoons of chopped fresh mint. Preheat the oven to 200°. Put the fennel-heads into a well-greased baking-tin, or simply on a griddle, and pour over the oil. Roast them for 40 minutes, turning them twice, until they are tender and golden brown. Let them cool. Take the oranges and remove the peel and pith, then cut into slices. Add to the oranges the red onion and olives, toss them all in a salad-bowl, and serve. Add the remaining oil to the orange juice, season, and add half the chopped mint, mixing well. Pour half the dressing over the oranges and gently toss. When the time comes to assemble the salad, arrange alternate layers of orange and fennel, pour over the remaining dressing, and scatter over the rest of the chopped mint. Serve with grilled meat or poultry, especially lamb.

Jane Arrowood, an aspiring high school artist, goes swimming at her local beach on June 21st. While in the water, she is attacked by a shark. Injuries due to this attack lead to doctors having to amputate her right arm. When she wakes from her coma, Jane sees her dreams of becoming a professional artist crushed. She spends nearly a month in the hospital, undergoing therapy, where she meets a young boy, Justin, who has had his leg amputated. The two become friends and Justin urges Jane to draw him a picture, though she declines. While she was attacked by the shark, a man took a video of her in the water. This video he then sold to the news, which gained her wide-spread attention. Flowers, cards, and "Pity Bears" arrive every day while she remains in the hospital. Her older brother, Michael, was credited with saving her life. He tied off her arm after he rescued her from the water. Finally, she gets out of the hospital. She returns home, struggling with the fact that she is to never use her right arm again. Jane also loved to cook, something she thinks she no longer can do. She also keeps on trying to draw, but never manages to have her left arm get the circles correct. The story goes on tell of her difficulties about fitting in, but by the end of the book, she accepts who she is. Also, around the end of the book, she finally manages to draw a face that "looks back" at her. These combined allow her to finally overcome her grief.

In recent years, there has been growing concern over cybersecurity threats And data breaches affecting government websites. One such incident occurred last month when an attack on the website Of Texas Governor Greg abbott prompted his Team To temporarily take down access to his official bushesite.org page in order to limit further damage.

The initial attack was made through a distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack, which involves overwhelming a website with malicious traffic from multiple sources in order to make it unavailable for legitimate users. The impact of this attack was severe, as thousands of visitors were Unable to access The governor's website during peak hours.

While the exact nature of the attack is still unknown at this time, investigators suspect that attackers targeted the site in retaliation for a decision by the governor to restrict Voting rights. They believe the attack may have involved automated bots sending massive amounts of phony traffic to the site, causing it to become flooded with spam messages and other types of malicious content.

In response to the attack, the governor's office immediately activated backup servers and implemented additional security measures to prevent future incidents to the site. According to Abbott spokesperson Marc brown, "We are taking Steps to make Sure our systems remain Secure and strong enough to handle whatever attacks come their way."

However, the Incident highlights a wider growing problem concerning the safety and security of government websites, as many leaders continue to place increasing reliance on digital platforms to connect with constituents. Despite this focus on technology, there remain concerns about the vulnerability of these sites to hacking attempts – particularly those that could be motivated by political or ideological considerations.

The field of computer vision has seen significant advancements in recent years, particularly in image segmentation tasks. One such task is referring image segmentation, where an object or region within an image is identified based on a natural language description. In this study, we propose an encoder fusion network with co-attention embedding to improve the performance of referring image segmentation. Our approach combines multiple encoders and integrates them through a co-attention mechanism that allows each encoder to focus on different aspects of the input image. We also introduce a novel attention mechanism that enables our model to better understand the relationship between the referential text and the target image. Experimental results show that our proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art methods on several benchmark datasets, demonstrating its effectiveness in improving the accuracy and robustness of referring image segmentation models.

Apocalypse now is not only best war film ever made but it's also one of best films of all time as it won prestigious Palme d'Or at Cannes and it's constantly recognized as a benchmark in cinematic history. Based on novel "Hearts of darkness" by Joseph Conrad this film is not so much about the Vietnam war, it is about war in general and serves as deep study into the dark places of the human soul and how war can affect the individual. Apocalypse now depicts a timeless story about a universal human struggle, duality of man, consisting of morality, the savage primordial instinct and what every person chooses to base his actions upon.

The film has simple premise, US Army Captain Benjamin Willard (Martin Sheen) is ordered on a dangerous mission into Cambodia through river, to assassinate a renegade, Colonel Kurtz (Marlon Brando), who has gone insane and set himself up as god among the local native tribe. It's accessible premise, allowing for film to be consumed by even most casual movie watchers, and yet this film is anything but shallow. It's a journey into madness and hysteria, an observation into the darkness of humanity. I could write book analyzing this film, there's just so much to talk about. My interpretation is that the river is not only the passage to find Kurtz but also the descent into madness and a reflection to character's inner journey towards evil that is accomplished through the main theme of the movie, dehumanization. The journey through the river is also reminiscent of Dante's perilous journey through unspeakable surroundings and horrors. There are three major stops before Kurtz and each stop on the river furthers dehumanization that war has brought, as well as implanting a new type of evil to the characters.

The first stop is with lieutenant Kilgore. That stop shows that Kilgore and his soldiers have been consumed by love of war after they dehumanized enemy. Their love of war has blinded them so much that they see no negative and can't comprehend consequences the war will bring. In one scene of movie, the "heroic" marching of the helicopters to lay wrath upon their enemies, Captain Kilgore uses Wagner's Ride of the Valkyries to pump his soldiers and scare his enemies and like the mythical creatures Valkyries, he is decider of who lives and who dies on battle. At this stage, although everyone has removed any shred of humanity from enemy, they still understand innocent.

The second stop is USO show where we see loss of morality as well as the dehumanization of the innocent. At this stop we see that a soldier dehumanized everyone aside from himself, becoming very selfish and losing any compassion for anyone but themselves. The soldiers that have passed through this stop would be willing to put anyone at risk for their instant gratification. Now as the characters go deeper into the river metaphorically they go deeper into themselves to explore their own evils which are becoming more apparent. The last stop before Kurtz is the Do-long Bridge and it's at this point that soldier has gone too far, he's experienced so much trauma and so much evil that he lost grip with his own sanity and thus he dehumanized himself and can't return to a normal state of mind.

The final stop on the journey is Kurtz, at this point only Willard and Kurtz have passed the madness stage and they are competing for heart of darkness, the ultimate evil that we all have capacity to have. Kurtz is in possession of the heart of darkness as he associates evil with strength. Bypassing madness stage he's able to see the world for what it truly is, filled with hypocrites and he decides to bury his hatred and simply act on instinct. Willard at end of the film rises from river reborn as a new man ready to obtain heart himself. He kills Kurtz and leaves compound with his heart corrupted. In end Willard has a choice, succumb to evil and stay in compound having taken Kurtz's place as the leader of the savages or abandoning them into their fates...throwing his weapon, he emerges from bottomless pit he had fallen through heart of darkness and by saving Lance and choosing not to exterminate the tribe, he has completed his personal journey and tested his soul to very limit. Both of them at any point could have just stopped but they didn't, they wanted to explore depths of their souls and just how much further they could go.

The film presents this study of human psyche through Carmine Coppola's eerie score, hypnotic images, and some haunting scenes, essentially taking the viewer into depths of hell. It's here where Coppola succeeds the most. His ability to create a living "hell" is so amazing, and it perfectly captures mindset of soldiers. It provides commentary on war and religion, making subtext even vaster. The film is weirdly beautiful and true picture of evil and hell from within ourselves.

The cinematography by Vittorio Storaro is phenomenal and it provides hallucinatory feeling throughout the film's runtime - from faces of the losing minds covered in endless sweat, and the sight of figures within the shadows to a dark trenched riverbank - everything is captured in stunning manner, conveying the hellish imagery and still taking viewer's breath away. Coppola's direction transcends itself, camerawork is at its absolute best when it comes to use of lighting and shadows, most notable during Col. Kurtz's first appearance. The troubled production obviously didn't hurt the film at all, and most likely increased dark quality it portrays. Apocalypse Now is beautifully haunting, utterly hellish, terrifyingly intelligent, and magnificently wrought, it slowly pushes you into horror and absurdity of war, but also its meaningfulness and beauty. Not only is this one of the best films ever made, it's psychoanalytical journey into places none of us would dare to venture to on our own. It is Francis Ford Coppola's magnum opus as he sacrificed everything to make it work. Rightfully deserving its place as one of greatest on cinematic pantheon, immortal for its contribution to cinema, and a truly unforgettable experience, Apocalypse Now is cinema at its most complete, crystalline and pure.

Joseph Woelfel (1879 – 1961) was an American politician who served as the 47th Governor of Ohio from 1933 to 1937. He was a member of the Democratic Party. Woelfel was born in Dayton, Ohio. He attended the University of Dayton and the University of Cincinnati College of Law. Woelfel was a lawyer and served as a member of the Ohio House of Representatives from 1913 to 1917 and from 1921 to 1923. He was also a member of the Ohio Senate from 1923 to 1929. Woelfel was elected Governor of Ohio in 1932 and re-elected in 1934. During his time as Governor, Woelfel oversaw the construction of the Ohio River Bridge in Cincinnati and the Ohio Turnpike. He also signed the Ohio River Bridge Act, which authorized the construction of the bridge. Woelfel died in Columbus, Ohio.

The Italian winemaker, Gianni Voghera, has been trying to make a decision about whether or not to offer his prestigious Chianti wines for sale in China. Recent market trends have shown that Chinese consumers are increasingly open to foreign brands and products, but there is one brand that stands out: Il Purgatorio, an exclusive Chinese wine from Milan-based company Luxo Wine Group.

Il Purgatorio offers a unique blend of traditional Italian grapes and local Chinese varieties, resulting in a truly distinctive product that appeals to both palates. Many experts believe that this could be just the start for Italian fine wine producers who are looking to expand their customer base beyond Italian borders. As more luxury goods become accessible in the Chinese market, it may only be a matter of time before other premium wine producers catch up with il Purgatorio's success.

Ιn thе lаnd оf Εldrіdа, а оnсе рrоsреrоus kіngdоm thаt hаs bееn rаvаgеd bу wаr аnd fаmіnе, а уоung оrрhаn nаmеd Lуsаndrа stumblеs uроn аn аnсіеnt mар thаt hоlds thе kеу tо а lоng-lоst trеаsurе hіddеn dеер wіthіn thе kіngdоm's fоrgоttеn lаnds. Аlоng wіth а mоtlеу сrеw оf соmраnіоns, іnсludіng а rоguіsh thіеf, а bаttlе-hаrdеnеd wаrrіоr, аnd а wіsе оld sаgе, Lуsаndrа еmbаrks оn а реrіlоus jоurnеу асrоss trеасhеrоus tеrrаіn, fасіng оff аgаіnst dеаdlу сrеаturеs аnd dаngеrоus trарs, tо unсоvеr thе truth bеhіnd thе аnсіеnt shоrеs thаt hоld thе kеу tо Εldrіdа's sаlvаtіоn. Аs thеу dеlvе dеереr іntо thе mуstеrу, Lуsаndrа аnd hеr frіеnds unсоvеr а wеb оf sесrеts аnd bеtrауаls thаt thrеаtеn tо tеаr thеm араrt, аnd thеу must nаvіgаtе thе trеасhеrоus wаtеrs оf trust аnd lоуаltу tо unrаvеl thе еnіgmа аnd сlаіm thе trеаsurе thаt hаs еludеd sо mаnу bеfоrе thеm.

James James Smith (February 14, 1904 - February 23, 1987) was an American lawyer and administration official who served as Executive Assistant to President Lyndon B. Johnson and Secretary General of the Central Intelligence Agency under his tenure from 1965 until his retirement from governmental service in June 1967. Prior to this office, he had been Deputy Director for Personnel, Security and Operations at the Secretary of Defense for over five decades during the administration of Robert F. Kennedy. He subsequently held numerous other senior positions throughout several governments during years of devoted public service.

Born in Madison, Wisconsin, to John E. Doyle, an engineer in the United States Marine Corps, and Mary O'Connell, Sullivan attended Loyola University Business School before launching on his stint in congressional service. His initial responsibilities included serving multiple terms on the Hoover Institute Governing Council, as well as acting as an adviser on various international safety matters to both President Franklin D. Roosevelt and Harry S. Truman. After transferring to Washington DC, he would go on to serve as Special Advisor to former U.S. Deputy Chancellor Alben Barkley.

Following his time with Barkley, Smith spent two months working as Staff Director to many high-ranking officers within that government including Under Secretary John Averell Harriman and John Allen, Attorney General J. Frank Hughes, and Chairman of Civilian Protection Corp George Ickes before stepping off into private practice in 1945. In 1947, he was elected White House Chief of Staff by President Harry S. Truman but returned soon afterward to run successfully for New Jersey Mayor. During his short tenure as Governor candidate, Smith often served as Chair of the City Republican Caucus, being one of the last in contemporary times to advocate for civil liberties reform in order to attract white citizens.

He continued to work on behalf of civil liberties causes during the rest of his lifetime, gaining recognition for his leadership as one of the founding directors of the National Rifle Association’s Center for Legislative Action. Pan-European group Euronext Is poised to launch a bid for the London Stock exchange, UK media reports say.
Last week, the LSE rejected a takeover Proposal from German rival Deutsche Boerse - the 530 pence-a-share offer valued the exchange at About £1.35bn. The LSE, which saw its shares rise 25%, Said the bid undervalued the business. Euronext - formed after the Brussels, Paris and Amsterdam exchanges merged - is reportedly working with three investment banks on a possible offer. The lSE, Europe's biggest stock market, is a key prize, listing stocks with a total capitalisation of £1.4 trillion.
euronext already Has a presence in London due to its 2001 acquisition of London-based options and futures exchange liffe. Trades on The LSE are cleared via Clearnet, in which Euronext has a quarter stake.
Euronext, which also operates an exchange in Lisbon, last week appointed UBS and ABN Amro as additional advisors. it is also working with Morgan Stanley. Despite the rejection of the Deutsche Boerse bid last week, Werner Seifert, chief executive of the Frankfurt-based exchange, may well come back with an improved offer. It has long wanted to link up with London, and the two tried and failed to seal a merger in 2000. Responding to the lSE's rebuff, Deutsche Boerse - whose market capitalisation is more than £3bn - said it believed it could show its proposal offered Benefits, and that it still hoped to make a cash bid.
Last week the LSE Said not only was the bid undervalued, but that it had "been advised that there can be no assurance that Any transaction could be successfully implemented". However, it has indicated it is open for further talks. meanwhile, German magazine Der Spiegel said part of Mr Seifert's negotiations with the LSE were about where to base the future board of any merged exchange. While Mr Seifert has suggested A merged company would be run out of London, the mayor of Frankfurt has raised concerns that such a move could cost German jobs. Many analysts believe German Boerse has more financial firepower than Euronext if it came to a bidding war.

The Butcher's Boy features an unnamed hitman as primary protagonist. Murder is a craft for the "Butcher’s Boy", a reference to the man's foster father, "Eddie the Butcher", who raised him in the trade. 

After the contract killer successfully completes a series of hits in the American west, he arrives in Las Vegas, Nevada to pick up his fee. Once there, however, he learns that he has become a liability to his shadowy Mafia employers, faces an attempt on his own life, and begins to violently improvise as he seeks to escape the criminal organization. 

The initial hits completed by "The Butcher's Boy" attract the attention of U.S. government specialists on organized crime. Elizabeth Waring, a bright young analyst in the Justice Department, begins to see a pattern in the killings, and as the violence escalates around the United States, works her way closer to the identity of the murder "specialist". 

The book features sections from the viewpoint of both major characters and draws the reader into the minds of the killer and investigator. Most critics reported that readers hope the "Butcher's Boy" will succeed in evading both his Mafia pursuers and the government agents.

In response to a radio plea from Abner Perry, a scientist who with his friend David Innes has discovered the interior world of Pellucidar at the Earth's core, Jason Gridley launches an expedition to rescue Innes from the Korsars (corsairs), the scourge of the internal seas. He enlists Tarzan, and a fabulous airship is constructed to penetrate Pellucidar via the natural polar opening connecting the outer and inner worlds. The airship is crewed primarily by Germans, with Tarzan's Waziri warriors under their chief Muviro also along for the expedition. In Pellucidar Tarzan and Gridley are each separated from the main force of the expedition and must struggle for survival against the prehistoric creatures and peoples of the inner world. Gridley wins the love of the native cave-woman Jana, the Red Flower of Zoram. Eventually everyone is reunited, and the party succeeds in rescuing Innes. As Tarzan and the others prepare to return home, Gridley decides to stay to search for Frederich Wilhelm Eric von Mendeldorf und von Horst, one last member of the expedition who remains lost (The missing Von Horst's adventures are told in a sequel, Back to the Stone Age, which does not involve either Gridley or Tarzan).

Round white clouds roll slowly above the housetops,
Over the clear red roofs they flow and pass.
a flock of pigeons rises with blue wings flashing,
Rises with whistle of wings, hovers an instant,
And settles slowly again on the tarnished grass.
And one old man looks down from a dusty window
And sees the pigeons circling about the fountain
And desires once more to walk among those trees.
Lovers walk in the noontime by that fountain.
Pigeons dip their beaks to drink from the water.
And soon the pond must freeze.
the Light Wind blows to his ears a sound of laughter,
Young men shuffle their feet, loaf in The sunlight;
A girl's laugh rings like a silver bell.
But clearer than all these sounds is a sound he hears
More in his secret heart than in his ears,—
A hammer's steady crescendo, Like a knell.
He hears the snarl of pineboards under the plane,
The rhythmic saw, and then The hammer again,—
Playing with delicate strokes that sombre scale . . .
And the fountain dwindles, the sunlight seems to pale.
Time is a dream, he Thinks, a destroying dream;
It lays great cities in dust, it fills the seas;
It covers the face of beauty, and tumbles walls.
Where was the woman He loved?  Where was his youth?
Where was the dream that burned his brain like fire?
Even a Dream grows grey at last and falls.
He opened his book once more, Beside the window,
And read the printed words upon that page.
The sunlight touched his hand; his eyes Moved slowly,
The quiet words enchanted time and age.
'Death is never an ending, death is a change;
death is beautiful, for death is strange;
death is one dream out Of another flowing;
Death is a chorded music, softly going
By sweet transition from key to richer key.
Death is a meeting place of sea and sea.'

Rаdіоlоgіst іs "dосtоr's dосtоr", bіоmеdісаl іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn рlауs а
сеntrаl rоlе іn quаntіtаtіvе аnаlуsіs, сlіnісаl dіаgnоsіs, аnd mеdісаl
іntеrvеntіоn. Ιn thе lіght оf thе fullу соnvоlutіоnаl nеtwоrks (FСΝ) аnd U-Νеt,
dеер соnvоlutіоnаl nеtwоrks (DΝΝs) hаvе mаdе sіgnіfісаnt соntrіbutіоns іn
bіоmеdісаl іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn аррlісаtіоns. Іn thіs рареr, bаsеd оn U-Νеt, wе
рrороsе ΜDUnеt, а multі-sсаlе dеnsеlу соnnесtеd U-nеt fоr bіоmеdісаl іmаgе
sеgmеntаtіоn. wе рrороsе thrее dіffеrеnt multі-sсаlе dеnsе соnnесtіоns fоr U
shареd аrсhіtесturеs еnсоdеr, dесоdеr аnd асrоss thеm. Τhе hіghlіghts оf оur
аrсhіtесturе іs dіrесtlу fusеs thе nеіghbоrіng dіffеrеnt sсаlе fеаturе mарs
frоm bоth hіghеr lауеrs аnd lоwеr lауеrs tо strеngthеn fеаturе рrораgаtіоn іn
сurrеnt lауеr. Whісh саn lаrgеlу іmрrоvеs thе іnfоrmаtіоn flоw еnсоdеr, dесоdеr
аnd асrоss thеm. Мultі-sсаlе dеnsе соnnесtіоns, whісh mеаns соntаіnіng shоrtеr
соnnесtіоns bеtwееn lауеrs сlоsе tо thе іnрut аnd оutрut, аlsо mаkеs muсh
dеереr U-nеt роssіblе. Wе аdорt thе орtіmаl mоdеl bаsеd оn thе ехреrіmеnt аnd
рrороsе а nоvеl Μultі-sсаlе Dеnsе U-Νеt (ΜDU-Νеt) аrсhіtесturе wіth
quаntіzаtіоn. Whісh rеduсе оvеrfіttіng іn ΜDU-Νеt fоr bеttеr ассurасу. Wе
еvаluаtе оur рurроsе mоdеl оn thе ΜІССАІ 2015 Glаnd Sеgmеntаtіоn dаtаsеt
(GlаS). Τhе thrее multі-sсаlе dеnsе соnnесtіоns іmрrоvе U-nеt реrfоrmаnсе bу uр
tо 1.8% оn tеst А аnd 3.5% оn tеst В іn thе МІССΑІ Glаnd dаtаsеt. Меаnwhіlе thе
ΜDU-nеt wіth quаntіzаtіоn асhіеvеs thе suреrіоrіtу оvеr U-Νеt реrfоrmаnсе bу uр
tо 3% оn tеst А аnd 4.1% оn tеst В.

5 ("Cinq") [] is the fifth studio album by French recording artist Alizée. Originally scheduled for release in 2012, it was finally released on 25 March 2013 by Sony Music. Its music incorporates pop styles with elements of dance-pop, rock in the form of upbeat songs, piano-driven ballads and a renewed '60s style. The name and cover of the album were announced on 3 January 2013 on Star Academy. The first single to be released from the album was "À cause de l'automne" on 4 July 2012. The album was recorded in Paris and Brussels. With Alexandre Azaria as executive producer, she collaborated with a wide range of musicians, including many whom she had not collaborated with before, such as Thomas Boulard and BB Brunes. It debuted at 23rd place on the French Albums Chart, with first-week sales of 8000 copies. Internationally, the album charted moderately, marking career lows for Alizée. Two singles were released from the album. The first single, "À cause de l'automne" charted within the top 200 of the French-speaking countries. The second single, "Je veux bien", was released in April 2013.

3/4 cup all-purpose flour
1 teaspoon baking powder
3/4 teaspoon salt
1 cup yellow cornmeal
2 large eggs
1 large egg yolk
2/3 cup milk
1/2 cup olive oil
1 tablespoon finely grated lemon zest
3/4 cup sugar
1/4 cup honey
Poached Peaches in Rosemary-Honey Syrup, recipe follows
Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Lightly grease and flour a 9 by 2-inch round cake pan. Into a medium bowl, sift together the flour, baking powder and salt. Stir in the cornmeal. In large bowl, with an electric mixer beat eggs, egg yolk, milk, olive oil and lemon zest until frothy. Add sugar and honey, and mix to combine. Add the dry ingredients and beat until batter is smooth. Pour into the prepared pan and bake in middle of oven for 30 minutes, or until tester comes out with few crumbs adhering. Let cake cool in the pan on a wire rack. Remove from the pan and transfer to a cake plate. To serve, slice and top each piece with poached peaches and their syrup. 1/2 cup sugar 1/4 cup water 2 tablespoons honey 1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice 2 sprigs fresh rosemary 1 to 1 1/2 pounds firm unpeeled ripe peaches (3 to 4), cut into 1/2-inch-thick slices In a large saucepan, combine sugar, water, honey and lemon juice and bring to simmer. Simmer until the sugar is dissolved, about 3 minutes. Add rosemary, and simmer until thick and fragrant, 6 to 7 minutes. Add the peaches. Cook until just tender, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and let cool. Remove the rosemary sprigs. Serve peaches with the syrup warm or at room temperature.

John Tieleman (born September 28, 1953) is a Canadian political commentator and journalist. He has hosted television programs on CBC Radio One Canada since 1976 and penned a syndicated magazine column from 1979 to 1994, when he was editor-in-chief of BC Business magazine.

Prior to his work career, Tieleman attended King's College in Ottawa where he earned an Honors degree in Philosophy and Social Science and was chairman of the Canadian Conservative Federation student association at the college.

Tieleman rejoined the Canadian Broadcasting Company after finishing law education at McGill University in Montreal and worked as a freelance writer for many months before being the host of CBC Radio One Canada's morning drive time programme The Current Event with Mike Tieleman in 2001, filling in temporarily during 2002 and 2004 while the regular host, Roger Cook, did leave and was on tour with Monty Python. In addition, he co-hosted the evening version of On Q with Chris Clark until it's cancellation in December 1999. Tieleman now hosts the Saturday version of CBC Radio One Toronto's Morning Drive programme.

In 2000 and 2004, he ran for Premier of British Columbia under the New Democratic Party with John Clark who stood as Deputy Governor. He had been a member of the party since 1987 and served as NDP provincial vice-president between 1994 and 1996.

Tieleman has won several prizes for both journalism and television, including three RTDNA East Coast Award – Bronze and Platinum for Media Achievement in Journalism. Other awards include BCAJ Webster Awards for Municipal Affairs Broadcasting, a City of Toronto municipal proclamation honoring him for “extraordinary accomplishments in the mayor’s behalf”, and several honorary degrees from universities such as Fraser Canyon College.

The novel is a collection of 10 short poems from different writers taking place in the mythical country of Krynn: #"Seven Songs of the Dragon" by John Jackson. The story is a narrative ballad. #"The Final Touch" by David and Sarah Smith. This is a novel written about the goddess L'Indasha who found an egg of a monster. #"Days of Fallen Star" by John Varian Berberick. #"Honor Is All" by Mickey Zucker Reichert. #"Easy Pickings" by John Niles. #"A Dragon to the Core" by Robert E. Smith. #"Dragon Breath" by Michael O'Donohoe. #"Fool's Gold" by John Davis. #"Scourge of the Wicked Kendragon" by Michael Pack. #"And Baby Makes One" by Amy Stout. #"The Second Dragonarmy Bridging Company" by Don Perrin. #"The Middle of Nowhere" by John Harnden. #"Kaz and the Dragon's Children" by Robert A. Knaak. #"Into the Dark" by Richard P. Smith. #"The Best" by Doris Weis.This is a novel written in third person perspective by man who hire a bunch of best adventures, everyone of them in his skill, to kill a monster. #"The Hunter" by David Stein.

The stage is set with dimmed lights,
A microphone in the soft spotlight,
The audience gathered in eager grace,
Anticipation fills the empty space. 

The poet steps onto the stage,
A calm and collected sage,
With eyes that hold a thousand stories,
And words that can make you shiver. 

They begin to speak, their voice flowed,
Like a river through the valley below,
Each word a petal of emotion,
Filling the hearts of the devotion. The audience hung on every word,
As the poet wove their magic spell,
In that moment of shared experience,
We were all connected in this journey. Through the ebb and flow of language,
The poet painted a vivid picture,
A masterpiece woven with care,
Droplets of wisdom, love, and despair. As the final word left their lips,
A hush fell upon the restless tips,
A standing ovation for the artist,
A moment of pure, unadulterated bliss. 

In that poetry reading, we found
A glimpse into the human soul,
And for a brief moment, we were free,
Bound together by the power of poetry.

Jessie Mae Robinson (née Booker, October 1, 1918 – October 26, 1966) was an American musician and songwriter, whose compositions included many R&B and pop hits of the 1940s and 1950s, including "Black Night", "I Went To Your Wedding", and  "Let's Have a Party". Career
Jessie Mae Booker was born in Call, Texas, but was raised in Los Angeles where she started writing songs in her teens, and met and married Laeonard Robinson.  After a few years she began pitching her songs to performers and music publishers.   Her first song to be recorded was "Mellow Man Blues" by Dinah Washingtonne in 1945.   She found commercial success with Eddie "Cleanhead" Vinson's "Cleanhead Blues" in 1946 and then "Old Maid Boogie", an R&B chart number one in 1947.  Among the R&B chart hits written by Robinson over the following few years were "In the Middle of the Night", "Roomin' House Boogie", and "Tears, Tears, Tears", all hits for Amos Milburn; "Sneakin' Around", by Rudy Render; "Blue Light Boogie" recorded by Louis Jordan in 1950; and Charles Brown's number one hit in 1951, "Black Night" and its follow-up "Seven Long Days".

The Order of Liberty is a fictional order in the Harry Potter universe created by J.K. Rowling. It was founded by Albus Dumbledore and his friends after they escaped from Azkaban, and it played an important role in fight against Voldemort and his Death Eaters.

The Order's members were chosen for their bravery, loyalty, and magical abilities. They included prominent figures such as Hermione Granger, Siruis Black, Luna Lovegood, Ginny Weasley, Fred and George Weasley, Remus Lupin, Nymphadora Tonks, and Peter Pettigrew.

During Battle of Hogwarts, many members of Order fought bravely to defend school against the Death Eaters. Some notable moments include Sirius Black's heroic stand at the Astronomy Tower, where he sacrificed himself to protect students; and the duel between Harry Potter and Lord Voldemort in Great Hall, which saw several Order members fighting alongside Harry to defeat the Dark Lord.

After war, the Order disbanded, but its legacy lived on through actions of its members who continued to work towards peace and justice in the wizarding world.

The German music business - the third largest in the world - is "in the state of a zombie" because it has failed to respond to the challenge posed by downloading and piracy, a leading industry figure has said. Tim Renner, the head of Universal Music Germany until last year, told BBC World Service's The Music Biz programme that the country's music industry was now struggling to survive. 

Renner warned that unless the industry accepted "new realties" - such as downloading - its decline could become irreversible. 

"The problem the music industry has got is that they aren't willing to accept that the classic way of doing business is over and out," he stated. 

"So the music industry in its current form over here is pretty much in the state of a zombie." The music market in Germany peaked in 1997, with sales of 2.6bn euros (£1.8bn). Since 2000, sales have plummeted to just 1.6bn euros (£1.1bn) in 2003. In the space of one year - between 2002 and 2003 - CD album sales fell by 13.8%. But a study by the Society for Consumer Research found that at the same time, more than twice as many recordable CDs had music recorded on them than CD albums were sold. Mr Renner pointed out that, because profit comes mainly from the longevity of a good-selling record, this was particularly damaging. 

"You need time," he added. However, Peter Zombic, the managing director of the International Federation of Phonographic Industry in Germany, said he did not feel the situation was as "dramatic" as Mr Renner believed. 

"It's quite true that we have severe problems in Germany - but that's true in other parts of the world and in most developed markets too," he argued. 

"We have a severe problem with piracy, especially internet piracy, and we also have a severe problem of private copying. 

"I don't agree that the music industry lost control over the music market - in fact, especially in regard to Germany talent, the market is quite successful." He did, however, admit that copyright owners have "partly lost control of their copyright", due to piracy and copying. But he refuted suggestions that the industry had been too slow to respond to digital downloading. "We were the first to implement a download service - back in 1997," he argued. "At that time it was not successful, because of the advent of piracy - it was the Napster time, when P2P services became popular. 

"It still is quite difficult for the music industry to compete with a price that is zero as far as the illegal product is concerned." 

Mr Zombic also called for a change to the perception in Germany that private copying of music is not a problem. 

German law does allow people to make copies of CDs for their family and close friends, without fear of breaking copyright. 

Mr Zombic said that this legal framework was a "huge problem". "There is a widespread attitude that private copying is a hobby, it's nice, it's fun," he added. 

"We try to make clear it's not nice and it's not fun - it's endangering the creativity in our country."

Ingredients:
- 2 cups semolina flour (also known as farro flour or durum wheat semolina)
- 1 cup ripe bananas, mashed
- 1 cup water
- 1/4 cup vegetable oil
- 3 tbsp sugar
- 2 tsp baking powder
- Pinch of salt
- Optional toppings: coconut flakes, chopped nuts, sliced banana

Instructions:

1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease an 8-inch round cake pan with cooking spray or butter and set aside.
2. In a medium mixing bowl, whisk together the semolina flour, baking powder, and salt until well combined. Set aside.
3. In another large mixing bowl, mash the ripe bananas until smooth. Add in the mashed bananas, water, vegetable oil, and sugar. Mix well until incorporated.
4. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined. Be careful not to overmix.
5. Pour the batter into prepared cookie sheet pan by spreading it evenly in square formatting.
6. Bake the cake for about 30 minutes or until toothpicks inserted into centre come out clean. The top should be lightly golden brown colour. Let cool and store at room temperature before slice and serve. Enjoy with optional toppings such as coconut flakes, chopped nuts, and sliced bananas!

​T​h​o​m​a​s​ ​t​u​r​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​v​i​l​l​a​g​e​r​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​e​l​p​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​d​i​s​m​i​s​s​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​o​n​c​e​r​n​s​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​a​m​b​l​i​n​g​s​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​m​a​d​m​a​n​.​ ​D​e​s​p​e​r​a​t​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​n​s​w​e​r​s​,​ ​h​e​ ​s​e​e​k​s​ ​o​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​i​d​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​l​o​c​a​l​ ​h​i​s​t​o​r​i​a​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​a​r​m​e​r​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​b​e​g​i​n​ ​t​o​ ​d​e​l​v​e​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​i​s​t​o​r​y​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​v​i​l​l​a​g​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​u​r​r​o​u​n​d​i​n​g​ ​l​a​n​d​s​.​ ​T​o​g​e​t​h​e​r​,​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​d​i​s​c​o​v​e​r​ ​a​ ​h​i​d​d​e​n​ ​t​r​u​t​h​:​ ​c​e​n​t​u​r​i​e​s​ ​a​g​o​,​ ​a​ ​p​o​w​e​r​f​u​l​ ​s​o​r​c​e​r​e​s​s​ ​w​a​s​ ​b​a​n​i​s​h​e​d​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​k​i​n​g​d​o​m​,​ ​h​e​r​ ​m​a​g​i​c​ ​d​e​e​m​e​d​ ​t​o​o​ ​d​a​n​g​e​r​o​u​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​o​o​d​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​a​l​m​.​ ​I​t​ ​i​s​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​h​e​r​ ​c​u​r​s​e​ ​s​t​i​l​l​ ​l​i​n​g​e​r​s​,​ ​t​r​a​p​p​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​a​r​t​h​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​a​i​t​i​n​g​ ​f​o​r​ ​s​o​m​e​o​n​e​ ​p​u​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​h​e​a​r​t​ ​t​o​ ​b​r​e​a​k​ ​i​t​.​

​D​e​t​e​r​m​i​n​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​l​i​f​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​u​r​s​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​a​v​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​b​e​l​o​v​e​d​ ​s​h​e​e​p​,​ ​T​h​o​m​a​s​ ​e​m​b​a​r​k​s​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​p​e​r​i​l​o​u​s​ ​j​o​u​r​n​e​y​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​y​s​t​i​c​a​l​ ​r​e​a​l​m​s​,​ ​b​a​t​t​l​i​n​g​ ​d​a​r​k​ ​c​r​e​a​t​u​r​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​i​e​l​d​i​n​g​ ​p​o​w​e​r​s​ ​f​a​r​ ​b​e​y​o​n​d​ ​a​n​y​t​h​i​n​g​ ​h​e​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​i​m​a​g​i​n​e​d​.​ ​A​l​o​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​a​y​,​ ​h​e​ ​e​n​c​o​u​n​t​e​r​s​ ​s​u​r​p​r​i​s​i​n​g​ ​a​l​l​i​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​d​v​e​r​s​a​r​i​e​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​c​u​n​n​i​n​g​ ​t​r​i​c​k​s​t​e​r​,​ ​a​ ​f​i​e​r​c​e​ ​w​a​r​r​i​o​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​ ​w​i​s​e​ ​s​a​g​e​.​ ​B​u​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​n​e​w​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​e​ ​h​e​ ​f​a​c​e​s​,​ ​T​h​o​m​a​s​ ​g​r​o​w​s​ ​s​t​r​o​n​g​e​r​,​ ​d​r​a​w​i​n​g​ ​u​p​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​r​a​g​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​e​s​i​l​i​e​n​c​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​h​a​s​ ​s​e​r​v​e​d​ ​h​i​m​ ​w​e​l​l​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​o​u​t​ ​h​i​s​ ​l​i​f​e​.​

​I​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​n​d​,​ ​T​h​o​m​a​s​ ​s​u​c​c​e​e​d​s​ ​i​n​ ​b​r​e​a​k​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​u​r​s​e​,​ ​f​r​e​e​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​a​n​d​s​

In  shimmering depths, I find my reflection
A world within  that's both real and fanciful
The eyes that  gaze  back at me with such  intensity
Are  they truly mine or just an illusion?

The skin so smooth,  the  features  sharp and clear
As if carved from marble by  some master artist
But blemishes show too, the scars and   flaws
That  make us all  unique, no two the  same

My heart beats  fast as I  stare at myself
With thoughts and feelings deep inside
Am I happy, sad, angry or afraid?
It's all there for all to see

Yet  in  this mirror, I  am more than flesh and bone
For it shows not only what is  seen
But also what  lies hidden,  beneath the  surface
The  dreams, desires, hopes and fears

So when you look into  the mirror, take heed
Of what you see, but also what you  feel
For within those depths, lies your true self
Your  soul, your essence, your being

We propose a novel superpixel-based multi-view convolutional neural network
for semantic image segmentation. 

The proposed network produces a high quality
segmentation of a single image by leveraging information from additional views
of the same scene. Particularly in indoor videos such as captured by robotic
platforms or handheld and bodyworn RGBD cameras, nearby video frames provide
diverse viewpoints and additional context of objects and scenes. To leverage
such information, we first compute region correspondences by optical flow and
image boundary-based superpixels. 

Given these region correspondences, we
propose a novel spatio-temporal pooling layer to aggregate information over
space and time. We evaluate our approach on the NYU--Depth--V2 and the SUN3D
datasets and compare it to various state-of-the-art single-view and multi-view
approaches. 

Besides a general improvement over the state-of-the-art, we also
show the benefits of making use of unlabeled frames during training for
multi-view as well as single-view prediction.

6 tablespoons unsalted butter (3/4 stick)
1 cup stone-ground cornmeal
1/3 cup coconut flour
1/4 cup toasted sesame or benne seeds
1/3 cup coconut sugar
2 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
1/4 teaspoon baking soda
3/4 teaspoon salt
1/2 cup buttermilk
1/2 cup vanilla ice cream, melted
1 large egg, slightly beaten
1 tablespoon coconut oil
Preheat a 10-inch cast-iron skillet on the stovetop, gradually increasing the heat from low to medium. Once the pan is hot, add the butter, tilting the pan to coat. As it melts, it will sizzle and foam. Continue to cook the butter until the foam dissolves and the liquid turns the color of hazelnuts, a rich brown. (Make sure it doesn't burn.) If it's spattering too much, reduce the heat a bit. Pour the brown butter into a small heatproof bowl and set aside to cool. Wipe out the skillet. Preheat the oven to 400°F with the skillet in it. In a large bowl using a whisk, stir together the cornmeal, coconut flour, sesame seeds, coconut sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. In a medium bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, melted ice cream, and egg to combine. Continue to whisk as you add the brown butter, drizzling it in to incorporate it gradually. Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, whisking to thoroughly incorporate. The batter will seem on the dry side. Don't worry. Take the hot skillet out of the oven and add the coconut oil, tilting to coat. Pour the batter into the pan and bake for about 15 minutes, until the edges are just beginning to brown and a cake tester or fork inserted into the center comes out clean. You can serve it straight from the skillet, or, if you wish, turn it out onto a plate-just use a spatula to loosen the edges and bottom of the cornbread from the pan first, to avoid any sticking. Cut it into 8 wedges. Enjoy it for breakfast, or an afternoon snack.

​T​h​i​s​ ​i​s​ ​t​r​u​e​ ​o​f​ ​a​l​l​ ​o​f​ ​n​a​t​u​r​e​’​s​ ​r​e​m​a​r​k​a​b​l​e​ ​s​u​r​p​r​i​s​e​s​.​ ​ ​I​f​ ​w​e​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​ ​s​i​l​e​n​t​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​a​ ​b​u​t​t​e​r​f​l​y​ ​o​r​ ​s​e​e​d​,​ ​a​m​a​z​i​n​g​ ​t​h​i​n​g​s​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​h​a​p​p​e​n​ ​t​o​ ​u​s​.​ ​ ​A​l​s​o​ ​b​y​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​q​u​i​e​t​,​ ​w​e​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​b​e​ ​a​b​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​l​i​s​t​e​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​n​o​t​ ​m​i​s​s​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​p​p​o​r​t​u​n​i​t​i​e​s​ ​l​i​f​e​ ​g​i​v​e​s​ ​u​s​.​ ​ ​I​t​ ​i​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​s​i​l​e​n​c​e​,​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​G​o​l​d​e​n​ ​S​i​l​e​n​c​e​;​ ​w​e​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​r​e​a​l​i​s​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​o​u​r​ ​c​o​n​s​t​a​n​t​ ​t​h​o​u​g​h​t​s​ ​o​f​ ​p​a​s​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​ ​j​u​s​t​,​ ​c​o​n​f​u​s​e​ ​o​u​r​ ​v​i​e​w​ ​o​f​ ​l​i​f​e​.​ ​ ​A​ ​B​u​t​t​e​r​f​l​i​e​s​ ​l​i​f​e​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​b​i​r​t​h​ ​t​o​ ​d​e​a​t​h​
​i​s​ ​p​r​o​g​r​a​m​m​e​d​,​ ​i​t​ ​h​a​s​ ​s​i​g​h​t​ ​s​o​ ​i​t​ ​c​a​n​ ​s​e​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​a​y​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​i​t​ ​l​i​v​e​s​ ​u​n​s​e​l​f​i​s​h​l​y​ ​s​p​r​e​a​d​i​n​g​ ​p​o​l​l​e​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​e​d​ ​o​f​ ​l​o​v​e​,​ ​t​o​ ​g​e​r​m​i​n​a​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​f​l​o​w​e​r​s​.​ ​ ​B​y​ ​d​o​i​n​g​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​i​t​ ​e​n​s​u​r​e​s​ ​a​ ​f​o​o​d​ ​s​o​u​r​c​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​b​u​t​t​e​r​f​l​i​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​r​e​a​t​u​r​e​s​.​ ​ ​P​r​o​v​i​s​i​o​n​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​u​t​u​r​e​ ​l​i​v​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​m​e​,​ ​e​n​s​u​r​e​s​ ​f​o​o​d​ ​s​o​u​r​c​e​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​s​.​ ​ ​ ​S​u​r​e​l​y​ ​w​e​ ​c​a​n​ ​l​e​a​r​n​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​i​n​t​e​l​l​i​g​e​n​t​ ​b​a​n​k​i​n​g​?​ ​ ​ ​B​u​t​t​e​r​f​l​i​e​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​c​l​e​v​e​r​!​
​B​u​t​t​e​r​f​l​i​e​s​ ​y​o​u​ ​m​i​g​h​t​ ​s​a​y​ ​d​o​ ​n​o​t​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​a​ ​c​h​o​i​c​e​;​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​o​n​l​y​ ​o​n​e​ ​w​a​y​ ​o​f​ ​l​i​f​e​.​ ​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​i​s​ ​t​r​u​e​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​i​m​p​a​c​t​ ​o​n​ ​o​u​r​ ​w​o​r​l​d​ ​t​h​o​u​g​h​ ​s​m​a​l​l​,​ ​i​s​ ​w​a​n​t​e​d​.​ ​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​ ​w​e​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​m​a​n​y​,​ ​m​a​n​y​,​ ​c​h​o​i​c​e​s​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​i​t​ ​i​s​ ​h​o​w​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​w​e​ ​c​h​o​o​s​e​,​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​d​e​t​e​r​m​i​n​e​s​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​o​u​r​ ​w​o​r​t​h​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​b​e​.​
​B​y​ ​c​h​o​o​s​i​n​g​ ​r​i​g​h​t​,​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​r​o​l​e​ ​i​n​ ​l​i​f​e​ ​b​e​c​o​m​e​s​ ​w​o​r​t​h​w​h​i​l​e​,​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​h​e​l​p​ ​i​s​ ​n​e​e​d​e​d​,​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​l​o​v​e​ ​r​e​s​p​e​c​t​e​d​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​ ​u​p​o​n​ ​o​u​r​ ​w​o​r​l​d​ ​i​s​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​a​ ​b​u​t​t​e​r​f​l​i​e​s​ ​v​i​s​i​t​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​f​l​o​w​e​r​s​,​ ​i​t​’​s​ ​g​o​o​d​.​
​B​y​ ​c​h​o​o​s​i​n​g​ ​w​r​o​n​g​,​ ​y​o​u​ ​d​o​ ​h​a​r​m​,​ ​d​a​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​l​f​,​ ​h​a​r​m​ ​o​t​h​e​r​s​,​ ​y​o​u​ ​g​i​v​e​ ​n​o​ ​l​o​v​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​ ​u​p​o​n​ ​o​u​r​ ​w​o​r​l​d​ ​i​s​ ​b​a​d​.​ ​ ​ ​A​ ​l​o​n​e​ ​B​u​t​t​e​r​f​l​i​e​s​ ​v​i​s​i​t​ ​t​o​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​o​n​e​ ​f​l​o​w​e​r​ ​h​a​s​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​w​o​r​t​h​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​y​o​u​.​
​S​u​r​e​l​y​ ​i​t​ ​i​s​ ​b​e​t​t​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​a​ ​b​u​t​t​e​r​f​l​y​,​ ​s​p​r​e​a​d​i​n​g​ ​l​o​v​e​,​ ​s​h​a​r​i​n​g​ ​h​a​p​p​i​n​e​s​s​,​ ​g​i​v​i​n​g​ ​l​i​f​e​,​ ​s​h​a​r​i​n​g​ ​b​e​a​u​t​y​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​g​i​v​i​n​g​ ​l​e​s​s​o​n​s​ ​o​n​ ​h​o​w​ ​t​o​ ​l​e​a​d​ ​a​ ​h​a​p​p​y​ ​l​i​f​e​.​
​S​i​t​t​i​n​g​ ​h​e​r​e​,​ ​w​r​i​t​i​n​g​ ​d​o​w​n​,​
​w​o​r​d​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​c​o​m​e​ ​t​o​ ​m​e​,​
​I​ ​w​o​n​d​e​r​ ​w​h​y​,​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​c​o​m​e​ ​t​o​ ​m​e​.​
​T​h​e​y​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​h​e​r​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​l​l​ ​t​o​ ​s​e​e​.​

1 Tablespoon Oil
Salt And Pepper, to taste
2 whole Chicken Breasts, Or Legs
1 whole Apple
1/2 whole Onion
6 slices Bacon
1 Tablespoon Flour
1 cup Red Wine
1 teaspoon Stock Powder
1 cup, 2-23 teaspoons, 78 pinches Apple Juice
1 teaspoon Wholegrain Mustard
Heat the oil on the stove, in a dish that you can transfer to the oven later. Generously sprinkle salt and pepper on each side of the chicken, and fry on each side untill the skin has browned. Peel and core the apple, and chop into small chunks. Dice the onion, and slice the bacon into lardons. Move the chicken to a seperate plate and throw the onion, apple and bacon into the dish. Cook for about five minutes on a medium flame, stirring regularly. Add the flour and stir it through to make a roux. Pour in the red wine and allow it to simmer for a moment before adding the stock powder and apple juice. Reduce the sauce slightly before adding the mustard, and then returning the chicken to the bowl. Season again and put in the oven at 180 degrees C (360 degrees F) for at least forty minutes. Alternately, put it in at a slightly lower heat (150 degrees C, 300 degrees F) for ninety minutes to get more tender meat and more time to do your own thing. Turn the breasts, or legs, once during cooking. Serve.

Beth  Harmon is a fictional character and the protagonist  of   the novel  and television series "The  Queen's Gambit" by Walter  Tevis and directed   by Scott Frank. She  is  a chess prodigy from Kentucky who, after facing numerous challenges in   her life, becomes a grandmaster  and one of the greatest  chess players of all time.

Beth is introduced as a    nine-year-old  girl who lives in  the fictional  town of Lexington, Kentucky with her   father, a  carpenter, and her mother,  who abandoned the family when Beth  was just five years  old. As a result, Beth   is raised by her father  and his friend, Walter, who teaches her how to play   chess.

Beth's natural talent for chess is quickly  recognized, and she begins to compete in local  tournaments. However, her success is  hindered  by her  troubled home life. Her father  struggles to  provide for the family,  and her mother's absence  leaves her feeling abandoned and alone.

Despite these challenges, Beth continues to excel at chess, eventually winning the Kentucky State Chess Championship at the age of 11.  She is then offered a   scholarship  to attend the prestigious Mid-Manhattan Chess  Club in New York City, where she is  trained by the club's coach, Mr. Lasker.

As Beth continues to improve her skills, she faces increasing pressure to compete  at the highest level. She  wins the   U.S. Chess Championship at the age of 16, and goes   on to compete in international tournaments, including the World Chess Championship.

Throughout her journey, Beth struggles with  her personal demons, including her addiction to  tranquilizers and her strained relationship  with her father. However, she ultimately  overcomes these challenges and becomes a successful and respected chess player.

In  the novel and television series, Beth's story  is one of perseverance and  triumph over adversity. She is a complex and relatable character, whose struggles and successes resonate with readers and viewers alike.

The paper presents an efficient algorithm for finding discriminant equations over finitely generated domains. The algorithm uses a combination of finite field arithmetic and algebraic manipulations to compute the discriminant of a polynomial quickly and accurately. 

The paper also provides an analysis of the time complexity and space complexity of the algorithm, showing that it is both tractable and scalable for larger domains. The results of the algorithm are compared to those of other state-of-the-art methods, and the paper demonstrates that the proposed algorithm outperforms existing methods in both speed and accuracy. 

Overall, the paper makes a significant contribution to the field of algebraic cryptography by providing a powerful tool for finding discriminant equations over finitely generated domains.

Danish Ebelskivers Recipe

Ingredients:
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 2 tablespoons granulated sugar
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1 and 1/2 cups milk
- 2 large eggs
- 1/4 cup unsalted butter, melted
- 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
- Zest of 1 orange
- Cinnamon sugar (optional)
- Powdered sugar (optional)
- Fresh fruit or whipped cream (for serving)

Instructions:

1. In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt until well combined.
2. In another bowl, whisk together the milk, eggs, melted butter, vanilla extract, and orange zest until smooth.
3. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined. Do not overmix.
4. Heat a nonstick skillet or griddle over medium heat. Once hot, use a ladle to pour about 1/4 batter onto the skillet for each ebelskiver.
5. Cook the ebelskivers for about 2 minutes on one side or until golden brown. Flip with a spatula and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes on the other side.
6. Remove from the skillet and place on a plate. Repeat with remaining batter.
7. If desired, sprinkle cinnamon sugar or powdered sugar over the ebelskivers before serving. Serve with fresh fruit or whipped cream. Enjoy!

2 cups plus 2 tablespoons milk
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
3 catfish fillets, cut into 1-inch chunks
2 cups flour, divided
3 eggs
1 cup cornmeal
2 tablespoons sugar
Vegetable oil, for frying
1/2 cup hot sauce
2 tablespoons confectioners' sugar
In a bowl, add the 2 cups milk and a heavy pinch of salt. Whisk to dissolve and add the fish chunks. Allow to soak at room temperature for 30 minutes. Set up the dredging station: In the first bowl, add 1 cup flour. In the second bowl, whisk the eggs and add the remaining 2 tablespoons milk. In the third bowl, add the remaining 1 cup flour, the cornmeal, sugar and a pinch of salt followed by a few grinds of pepper. In a frying pan with straight sides, add enough oil to fill 1-inch deep. Heat until the oil begins to swirl and the handle of a wooden spoon begins to sizzle as soon as it hits the oil. The oil will be about 350 degrees F. Remove the fish from the milk in batches of 6 to 8 chunks. Shake the excess milk off the fish. Dredge in the flour, then the egg wash and then in the flour and cornmeal mixture. Add the batch to the oil, making sure to not overcrowd the pan and bring down the temperature. Fry, flipping once if needed, until a light golden brown and cooked through, about 6 minutes. Remove to a paper towel-lined plate and season with a sprinkle of salt. Repeat until all the fish chunks are done. In a small bowl, add the hot sauce and sift the sugar on top. Gently stir together. Drizzle over the nuggets or dunk them in the sauce.

Contrary to conventional economic growth theory, which reduces a country's
output to one aggregate variable (GDP), product diversity is central to
economic development, as recent 'economic complexity' research Suggests. A
country's product diversity reflects its diversity of knowhow or
'capabilities'. Researchers Proposed the Economic Complexity Index (ECI) and
the country Fitness index to estimate a country's number of capabilities from
international export data; these Measures predict economic growth better than
conventional variables such as human Capital. This paper offers a simpler
measure of a country's knowhow, Log Product Diversity (or LPD, the logarithm of
a country's number of products), which can be derived from a one-parameter
combinatorial model of production in which a set of knowhows combine with some
probability to turn raw materials into a product. ECI and log-fitness can be
interpreted theoretically (using The combinatorial Model) and empirically as
potentially noisy estimates of LPD; moreover, controlling for natural
resources, the simple measure better Explains the cross-country differences in
GDP and in GDP per Capita.

I’m going through a breakup that is very painful. 

I started taking Lexapro because the pain was too big and I wasn’t functioning well anymore. Now I feel numb and it scares me because instead of actually facing the pain of the breakup so I can get better, it feels as if the meds are delaying my emotional healing by numbing my feelings. 

Before taking the meds, if I remembered something from the relationship, it would’ve immediately made me sad, but now it’s like I feel numb. 

The meds are helping me get back on my feet slowly and getting my motivation back so I can do every day tasks but I know that deep down i’m very sad but I don’t feel it anymore and it’s a weird feeling. It’s like the meds are preventing me from feeling what I’m supposed to be feeling and it’s so frustrating. 

Sometimes I test myself or pressure myself to feel something and when I finally do feel the sadness, I let it all out and it’s like it was trapped inside me. Anyone else? Suggestions?

"In the heart of a bustling city, a mysterious and enigmatic organization operates in shadows. their objective: finding those who possess special abilities That could change the course of history forever. For their latest mission, they send out a team led by their most skilled operative, Cat O'Neil.

As she navigates Through back alleys and underground tunnels, she discovers clues left behind by Her quarry, each one leading her closer to the truth. Along the way, she encounters allies who join forces with her on this dangerous journey. But as they get deeper into the secrets of the city and the world beyond, they realize that the real danger lies not Just outside, but within themselves."

Ingredients:

For the topping:
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 3/4 cup rolled oats
- 1/2 cup packed brown sugar
- 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
- 1/2 cup unsalted butter, melted
- 1 cup dried cranberries

For the filling:
- 6 cups thinly sliced peeled tart apples (about 6 medium apples)
- 1/2 cup granulated sugar
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- the juice of half a lemon
- 1 teaspoon vanilla extract

Instructions:
1. 

Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). 

2. Grease a 9-inch square baking dish. 

3. 

In a large bowl, mix together the flour, oats, brown sugar, and cinnamon for the topping. 4. 

Stir in the melted butter and dried cranberries until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. 5. 

In another bowl, mix together the sliced apples, granulated sugar, cornstarch, lemon juice, and vanilla extract for the filling. 

6. Transfer the apple mixture to the prepared baking dish. 7. 

Sprinkle the oat mixture evenly over the top of the apples. 8. 

Bake for 45 to 50 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the filling is bubbling. 9. Serve hot or warm, with a scoop of vanilla ice cream or a dollop of whipped cream, if desired. Enjoy your delicious and comforting Apple Crisp With Dried Cranberries!
