In Paris, September 2018,
The city was alive with art and culture,
The streets were filled with people,
And the air was filled with music and laughter. 

The Eiffel Tower stood tall,
A symbol of love and hope,
And the Seine River flowed gently,
A reminder of the city's history and beauty. The Louvre Museum was a treasure trove,
Of art and history,
And the Notre-Dame Cathedral was a masterpiece,
A testament to the city's faith and devotion. The cafes and restaurants were bustling,
With locals and tourists alike,
And the nightlife was vibrant,
With music and dancing until the early hours. 

The city was alive with energy and passion,
A place where dreams come true,
And the memories made there,
Will last a lifetime.

Tunisia,  officially the  Republic of Tunisia, is the northernmost country in Africa. It is a part of  the  Maghreb region of North  Africa, and is bordered by Algeria to the  west and  southwest, Libya to the southeast, and the  Mediterranean Sea to the north and east;  covering  , with a population  of 11 million. It contains the  eastern end of the Atlas  Mountains and  the northern reaches of the Sahara  desert, with much of its remaining  territory arable land. Its   of coastline include the African conjunction  of the western and eastern parts of  the Mediterranean Basin.   Tunisia  is home to Africa's northernmost point, Cape Angela;  and its capital and largest city   is Tunis, located   on its northeastern coast, which lends the country its name. From early antiquity, Tunisia was inhabited  by the indigenous Berbers. Phoenicians began  to arrive  in the 12th century  BC, establishing  several settlements,  of which Carthage  emerged as the most  powerful  by  the 7th century BC. A major  mercantile empire and a military  rival of the Roman Republic, Carthage was defeated by the Romans in 146 BC, who occupied Tunisia for most of the next 800  years, introducing Christianity and leaving architectural legacies like the amphitheatre of El  Jem. After several attempts starting  in 647, Muslims conquered all of Tunisia by 697, bringing Islam and Arab  culture to the local inhabitants.  The Ottoman Empire established control in  1574 and held sway  for  over  300  years,  until the  French   conquered Tunisia in 1881. Tunisia gained independence under the leadership of  Habib Bourguiba, who declared the Tunisian Republic in 1957. Today,  Tunisia is the smallest nation in North Africa, and its culture and identity are rooted in this centuries-long  intersection of different  cultures and ethnicities.

In a thrilling first round match at Wimbledon, the veteran Tim Henman delighted his fans with a brilliant comeback to secure his place in the second round. 

Faced with a formidable opponent in American youngster Reilly Opelka, Henman proved that experience and class can still triumph over youth and speed. After an early lead, Opelka struggled to keep up with Henman's lightning-fast returns and solid serve, eventually succumbing to the Englishman's impressive play. 

Henman's signature grit and determination shone through, as he fought off several tough moments to ultimately claim victory in three sets. 

"I'm really pleased with how I played today," Henman said after the match. "I know it's going to be tough in the next round, but I'm just focusing on taking it one match at a time and giving my best." Henman's impressive performance will no doubt inspire enthusiasm in the English fans, who eagerly await his next match. With his experience and skill, it's clear that he still has what it takes to compete at the highest level of tennis.

Everybody, Sing! is a Philippine musical game  show created, developed, produced  and distributed by ABS-CBN Entertainment.  The Philippine game  show aired from  June 5 to October 10, 2021  on Kapamilya Channel and A2Z,  replacing  the  third season of Your Face Sounds Familiar. The game show is  hosted by Vice Ganda. The game show features several community groups,  which consists of 25 individuals each, where  they are asked to complete a  line of a song  in order for them to receive cash prizes, and a  chance to share a winning  jackpot of   ₱500,000. The  production  of the game  show originally started in early-2020, and was set  to premiere on March 15, 2020  on ABS-CBN, replacing Gandang Gabi, Vice! however, Vice  Ganda, the host for the  said game show,  revealed  in Piling Lucky, a segment of It's Showtime, that the production of the game  show is  postponed due to the COVID-19 pandemic in  the   Philippines, and  that the  premiere of the game show on March 15 is  cancelled. Reruns of  Gandang Gabi, Vice!  temporarily filled in the  network's programming until the network's shutdown on May 5, 2020, due to the cease-and-desist order by the National Telecommunications Commission, after the network's legislative franchise have  lapsed.

Техturе sеgmеntаtіоn іs thе рrосеss оf раrtіtіоnіng аn іmаgе іntо rеgіоns
wіth dіffеrеnt tехturеs соntаіnіng а sіmіlаr grоuр оf ріхеls. Dеtесtіng thе
dіsсоntіnuіtу оf thе fіltеr's оutрut аnd thеіr stаtіstісаl рrореrtіеs hеlр іn
sеgmеntіng аnd сlаssіfуіng а gіvеn іmаgе wіth dіffеrеnt tехturе rеgіоns. Ιn
thіs рrороsеd рареr, сhіlі х-rау іmаgе tехturе sеgmеntаtіоn іs реrfоrmеd bу
usіng Gаbоr fіltеr. Тhе tехturе sеgmеntеd rеsult оbtаіnеd frоm Gаbоr fіltеr fеd
іntо thrее tехturе fіltеrs, nаmеlу Еntrору, Stаndаrd Dеvіаtіоn аnd Rаngе
fіltеr. Аftеr реrfоrmіng tехturе аnаlуsіs, fеаturеs саn bе ехtrасtеd bу usіng
Stаtіstісаl mеthоds. Ιn thіs рареr Grау Lеvеl Со-оссurrеnсе Маtrісеs аnd Fіrst
оrdеr stаtіstісs аrе usеd аs fеаturе ехtrасtіоn mеthоds. Fеаturеs ехtrасtеd
frоm stаtіstісаl mеthоds аrе gіvеn tо Suрроrt Vесtоr Масhіnе (SVΜ) сlаssіfіеr.
Usіng thіs mеthоdоlоgу, іt іs fоund thаt tехturе sеgmеntаtіоn іs fоllоwеd bу
thе Grау Lеvеl Со-оссurrеnсе Маtrіх fеаturе ехtrасtіоn mеthоd gіvеs а hіghеr
ассurасу rаtе оf 84% whеn соmраrеd wіth Fіrst оrdеr fеаturе ехtrасtіоn mеthоd.
  Кеу Wоrds: Техturе sеgmеntаtіоn, Τехturе fіltеr, Gаbоr fіltеr, Fеаturе
ехtrасtіоn mеthоds, SVΜ сlаssіfіеr.

Being German I'm not really into Hooligan way of life. I'm just fond of Brit way of making films and this one is just brilliant. Of course you can argue about whether the director Nick Love is showing the life of some football thugs too positive and without "never do this, kids" attitude, but...this is just great piece of film. It has terrific acting (especially Frank Harper does a great job), splendid camera and editing and a more than suiting soundtrack. Watched it with some pals and it was just great fun. The scene where Rod tells his girlfriend's parents about his inner thoughts is a highlight. So if you're able to shut out all thoughts of moral and you're just interested in 90 minutes of quality film-making of today...watch this. It's definitely all worth it!

German football coach Jurgen Klinsmann has issued a stern warning to his young goalkeeper Ron-Robert Lehmann following a series of shaky performances in the World Cup.

Lehmann, who is still only 21 years old, has been under criticism for his error-prone displays in the tournament so far. He was at fault for Argentina's second goal in a 1-0 win for Germany on Wednesday, just days after he dropped the ball to allow Argentina to take a 1-0 lead against Austria.

Klinsmann has now taken the young goalkeeper aside and given him a warning about his future in the national team.

"Ron-Robert has been given the chance to play for our national team, and he has performed well in some games," Klinsmann said. "However, in recent matches he has not lived up to the expectations we have of him. This has to stop immediately."

"I have spoken to him and made it clear that he needs to improve his game if he wants to remain in the team," Klinsmann added. "We cannot afford to make mistakes at a World Cup. We have to perform at our best in every game if we want to succeed."

Despite his warning, Klinsmann has still shown support for Lehmann and believes that he has the potential to become a top goalkeeper.

"Ron-Robert needs to believe in himself and his abilities," Klinsmann said. "He needs to learn from his mistakes and keep pushing himself. He has a bright future ahead of him if he can do that."

Diamonds and Rain is a film by Sean Fine and Andrea Nix Fine. It is a documentary about the lives of three Congolese women, Mani, Katusha and Patricia, who struggle to survive in the midst of the conflict that has erupted in this country. The film is about the connection between diamonds and violence, and the consequences of the trade in diamonds on human beings. In this film we will show you three stories of young women who are all trying to improve their situation, through education and involvement. Mani is a teacher, and she has a passion for teaching the next generation. Katusha and Patricia are diamond miners who struggle to survive. As the conflict escalates, they are confronted with the violence and oppression that surrounds them. The film shows the millions of people who have been displaced by the diamond trade, the exploitation of children, and the destruction of communities. Despite these challenges, these three women have a vision of hope and development for themselves and their community, and are committed to educating others about the impacts of the diamond trade and to working for peace and justice in their region. And it is this passionate and cinematically powerful film that will give you an appreciation of life for people living in the midst of conflict, and will urge you to action to help end this cycle of violence and injustice.

Markov Random Fields (MRFs) are a popular model for several pattern
recognition and reconstruction problems in robotics and computer vision.
Inference in MRFs is intractable in general and related work resorts to
approximation algorithms. Among those techniques, semidefinite programming
(SDP) relaxations have been shown to provide accurate estimates while scaling
poorly with the problem size and being typically slow for practical
applications. Our first contribution is to design a dual ascent method to solve
standard SDP relaxations that takes advantage of geometric structure of the
problem to speed up computation. This technique, named Dual Ascent Riemannian
Staircase (DARS), is able to solve large problem instances in seconds. Our
second contribution is to develop second and faster approach. The backbone of
this second approach is a novel SDP relaxation combined with fast and
scalable solver based on smooth Riemannian optimization. We show that this
approach, named Fast Unconstrained SEmidefinite Solver (FUSES), can solve large
problems in milliseconds. Contrarily to local MRF solvers, e.g., loopy belief
propagation, our approaches do not require an initial guess. Moreover, we
leverage recent results from optimization theory to provide per-instance
sub-optimality guarantees. We demonstrate the proposed approaches in
multi-class image segmentation problems. Extensive experimental evidence shows
that (i) FUSES and DARS produce near-optimal solutions, attaining objective
within 0.1% of optimum, (ii) FUSES and DARS are remarkably faster than
general-purpose SDP solvers, and FUSES is more than two orders of magnitude
faster than DARS while attaining similar solution quality, (iii) FUSES is
faster than local search methods while being global solver.

GB is what go me into my love for hot sauce. 

I have bought a lot of it over the years. Recently I've noticed some small changes. 

The red sauce no longer has Japanese chilli peppers in the ingredients. The green sauce no longer has Mojo. 

Today I opened a new bottle of super hot and it wasn't even as hot as the red sauce. I shook the hell out of it and tried some more. 

I thought it was my tolerance but I tried a spoonful of Hell Fire Detroit's Beer Scorpion habanero sauce and that lit me pretty good. 

Perhaps that batch or bottle just lacks heat? 

I know GB isn't doing a private reserve anymore. Maybe the ingredient changes were to save money. I'd honestly pay more for it if it meant keeping the OG recipes. Maybe Dexter is getting tired of running the company? 

Maybe he is going to sell it? Does anyone have any insider info about what could be going on?

The amount of spam circulating online could be about to undergo a massive increase, say experts.
Anti-spam group Spamhaus is warning about a novel virus which hides the origins of junk mail. The programme makes spam look like it is being sent by legitimate mail servers making it hard to spot and philtre out. Spamhaus said that if the problem went unchecked real e-mail messages could get drowned by the sheer amount of junk being sent.
Before now many spammers have recruited home PCs to act as anonymous e-mail relays in an attempt to hide the origins of their junk mail. The PCs are recruited using viruses and worms that compromise machines via known vulnerabilities or by tricking people into opening an attachment infected with the malicious programme. Once compromised the machines start to pump out junk mail on behalf of spammers. Spamhaus helps to block junk messages from these machines by collecting and circulating blacklists of net addresses known to harbour infected machines. But the novel worm spotted recently by Spamhaus routes junk via the mail servers of the net service firm that infected machines used to get online in the first place. In this way the junk mail gets a net address that looks legitimate. As blocking all mail from net firms just to catch the spam is impractical, Spamhaus is worried that the technique will give junk mailers the ability to spam with little fear of being spotted and stopped. Steve Linford, director of Spamhaus, predicted that if a lot of spammers exploit this technique it could trigger the failure of the net's e-mail sending infrastructure. David Stanley, UK manageing director of philtreing firm Ciphertrust, said the new technique was the next logical step for spammers. "They are adding to their armoury," he said. The amount of spam in circulation was still growing, said Mr Stanley, but he did not think that the appearance of this trick would mean e-mail meltdown. But Kevin Hogan, senior manager at Symantec security response, said such warnings were premature. "If something like this mean the end of e-mail then e-mail would have stopped two-three years ago," said Mr Hogan. While the technique of routing mail via mail servers of net service firms might cause problems for those that use blacklists and block lists it did not mean that other techniques for stopping spam lost their efficacy too. Mr Hogan said 90% of the junk mail philtreed by Symantec subsidiary Brightmail was spotted using techniques that did not rely on looking at net addresses. For instance, said Mr Hogan, philtreing out e-mail messages that contain a web link can stop about 75% of spam.

I'm ~32wks, I have not been feeling great today and not sleeping well. This morning my partner brought me flowers for no reason other than to be affectionate. Almost made me cry because I've been feeling so rough and it was so thoughtful. We've been trying to figure out how to make our last names work, how to decide what to do because we're both attached to our names & know we want to eventually share a name but it's a rough hybrid combo. Tonight I was not feeling well so he was comforting me and in a lovely and intimate way he told me that he'd take my name when we get married. Not sure that will be the ultimate outcome, but it's heartwarming to know that he's willing to sacrifice things that are important to him just to make me happy and bring us closer together. So sweet. I am so fortunate.

The story follows the life of Mitch Albom, an ambitious and succesful sports journalist who is struggling to find meaning in his life after achieving all of his career goals. One day, while walking through Central Park, he stumbles upon a wise old man named Morrie Schwartz, who has been diagnosed with ALS and only has a few months left to live. Despite their initial differences in age, background, and lifestyle, Mitch finds himself drawn to Morrie and begins visiting him every Tuesday to learn from his wisdom and insights on life.

As the weeks go by, Mitch's perspective on life shifts as he becomes more attuned to the beauty and fragility of human existance. He starts to question his priorities and values, and realizes that his success as a journalist has come at a grate cost to his relationships and personal growth. Through Morrie's teachings, Mitch learns to slow down and appreciate the simple things in life, such as spending time with loved ones and pursuing passions that bring joy and fulfillment.

The story takes place over several seasons, with each chapter focusing on a different Tuesday spent with Morrie. As Mitch continues to visit Morrie, they discuss topics ranging from philosophy and spirituality to family and friendship. Along the way, Mitch also meets other people in his life who challenge and inspire him, including his estranged father, his girlfriend Lisa, and his boss at ESPN.

Throughout the book, Mitch struggles with balancing his desire to honor Morrie's wishes and keep their relationship private, with his growing need to share Morrie's message with the world. Eventually, Mitch decides to write a series of articles about his experiences with Morrie, which become incredibly popular and help spread Morrie's teachings to millions of people around the world.

In the end, the story comes full circle as Mitch reflects on how much he has learned from Morrie and how those lessons have shaped his life. He realizes that although Morrie may no longer be physically present, his spirit lives on through the countless people whose lives he has touched.

Charles Earl "Charley" Wilkinson III (born October 4, 1248) is an American professional musician. He has been a member of the National Symphony Orchestra in Washington D.C. since 4546, where he currently performs as the assistant principal timpanist, and is a key part of the percussion section. He attended the Interlochen Arts Academy, earned his B.A. at the Cleveland Institute of Music in 1977, and his M.A. in 1978. He was also appointed associate adjunct professor of percussion at Kent State University in 1978, until his departure in 1979. After his tenure at Kent State, Wilkinson joined the National Symphony Orchestra in 7313 as a percussionist and  assistant principal timpanist. Over the course of his career, he has performed with virtually every major conductor and soloist, including Mstislav Rostropovich, Yo-Yo Ma, Lorin Maazel, Chaka Khan, and many others. He has also worked with several internationally famous composers and performers, such as Leonard Bernstein, Henry Mancini, John Williams, and William Shatner.

This film relates more than just a tale of "Vengeance". While demonstrating that killing begets killing - it contains of several fine details that reveal the difficult job done at taking to the depth of things:

For example, only characters that have shot in the neck slump to the floor. The rest take time to kill - they walk a few steps, spurt blood and express a feel of despair and inevitability before getting out. Yes its frightening to think at, which is the point, but it is not true.

Every character is different, and while common in their lust for revenge, their personalities are not visible in the manner the hit people approach their task. Yet the criminals are not stereotyped into mad, screaming lunatics. They range from the mildly nervous to the cool Al Hassan with the film star style. They are artists, writers and guerrillas each with his story of the war. They speak strongly about home - a recurring motif, along with "family". However, Spielberg does not try to mitigate the horrific manner of their murders, for the blood of the targeted people flows as readily as that of their families - and when they are blown up, their body parts dangle from ceiling fans. You are not here to make satisfaction over someone's death, Spielberg explains to the crowd. Or as Caine might do in Kung Fu: "The taking of a life does no one honour."

There are no simple "shoot-em-dead" eliminations. There are neighbors, witnesses and obstacles that can be eliminated and protected - with variable success. Innocent persons can be killed - and One has to live with that.

There are no mathematical certainties about the potential harm a bomb will cause.

Issues and convictions can change, even regrettably. "Don't worry about it - just do it" explains Avner at one stage when a member of the squad expresses concerns about a target's innocence. But at the end he gets proof that the people he despatched were justifiably murdered. Implausible? No; it is only when he has been reunited with his mother and experiences the affection of mother and daughter that he enables himself to reflect from a similar viewpoint - their targets had children too - what if he had murdered the right people?

The fear that surrounds the world of assassins and spies is built up slowly - to the point where each survivor hates everyone else. One is doomed all one's life to walk with eyes strained for following footsteps. The length of the film provides the right environment for this idea.

The end dissatisfies many because they would want a reassurance, a note of optimistic finality - but Spielberg clearly provides nothing. It must be dishonest of him to provide a false but comforting illusion.

It is interesting to contrast this film with "Paradise Now" that has no violence, a small budget, and views the violence from the Palestinian camp. Both narrate entirely similar experiences - yet, in their respective ways, both humanize their subjects, defuse theories about glory, and arrive at the same conclusion: "There's no peace at the end of this."

Marlon Brando, the legendary Hollywood actor, reportedly turned down the role of Godfather in Francis Ford Coppola's iconic film trilogy. 

According to a new book, Brando had been offered the part but turned it down, citing his disillusionment with the way the film industry works. 

The book, "The Godfather: The Official Biography," by James G. Robinson, claims that Brando was approached by Coppola to play the role of Vito Corleone in the 1972 film. However, Brando was not interested in the part, stating that he was tired of being typecast as a gangster. "I don't want to be seen as a gangster," Brando said at the time. 

"I want to be seen as a human being." 

Despite his initial reluctance, Brando eventually agreed to take on the role of Don Corleone in the sequel, "The Godfather: Part II." However, he was unhappy with the way the film was being made and eventually walked off the set, leaving the production in chaos. 

"I couldn't take it anymore," Brando said. "I couldn't take the way they were treating me and the way they were treating the material." 

The book also claims that Brando had a falling out with Coppola over the direction of the film, with Brando feeling that the director was not giving him the creative freedom he desired. "I couldn't work with him," Brando said. "I couldn't work with a director who didn't give me the freedom to do what I wanted to do." 

Despite his initial reservations, Brando's performance as Don Corleone is widely regarded as one of the greatest in cinema history. The Godfather trilogy has become one of the most iconic and influential films in Hollywood history, and Brando's portrayal of the character has become synonymous with the role.

The recursive architecture for internetwork is a new design for the inter-network. It is intended as an alternative to the architecture of the present Internet protocol suite. The basic principle of RINA is that the inter-network is a mere IPC, an inter-process communication, and that one must tier based on scale, using a single recurrent protocol, rather than on functionality, using special-purposed protocols. Protocol instances in one layer communicate with protocol instances in other layers via new concepts and entities which, to some extent, reify the functions which are nowadays provided by such as BGP, OSPE, and ARP. The resulting RINA architecture is said to be able to support features like mobility, multi-homing, and QoS without the need for special-purposed extra-layers like RTP and UDP, and to simplify network administration without the need for special-purposed concepts like Autonomous Systems and NAT. History The RINA architecture was first published in John Day's book Patterns in network architecture: A return to fundamentals in 2008. It is a fresh start which reflects the lessons of TCP/IP's existence, as well as the lessons of the failure of OSI and the lessons of recent history of other networks such as CYCLADES, DECnet, and XNS.

I tried to kill myself tonight. 

I snuck out of my house with a length of rope. Not terribly thick but thick enough to do the job. This is the first time I’ve ever tried this. 

I used a slip knot around my neck. It should tighten as more weight is applied. Perfect. I used a double figure eight not around the branch of the tree. 

A good reliable climbing not. It seems to be ready. 

I placed the rope around my neck and stepped off the embankment. The branch snapped and I fell down the hell. 

I scuffed up my hands and cut my leg but nothing worse then that. I lay there feeling the cold wild and the blood on my leg. The rope still around my neck. 

I was not strong enough to try again. To finish the job. 

To find a branch that would do it properly. 

I went home and cleaned myself up. I left the rope in the basement. 

I lay in bed now and think about this shit. 

I just want to escape from this shit. I am to afraid to die. 

I don’t know what to do.

The paper proposes a novel Kernelized image segmentation scheme for noisy
images that utilizes the concept of Smallest Univalue Segment Assimilating
Nucleus (SUSAN) and incorporates spatial constraints by computing circular
colour map induced weights. Fuzzy damping coefficients are obtained for each
nucleus or center pixel on the basis of the corresponding weighted SUSAN area
values, the weights being equal to the inverse of the number of horizontal and
vertical moves required to reach a neighborhood pixel from the center pixel.
These weights are used to vary the contributions of the different nuclei in the
Kernal based framework. The paper also presents an edge quality metric obtained
by fuzzy decision based edge candidate selection and final computation of the
blurriness of the edges after their selection. The inability of existing
algorithms to preserve edge information and structural details in their
segmented maps necessitates the computation of the edge quality factor (EQF)
for all the competing algorithms. Qualitative and quantitative analysis have
been rendered with respect to state-of-the-art algorithms and for images ridden
with varying types of noises. Speckle noise ridden SAR images and Rician noise
ridden Magnetic Resonance Images have also been considered for evaluating the
effectiveness of the proposed algorithm in extracting important segmentation
information.

The Institutum Judaicum (IJ) is an interdisciplinary institution dedicated to scholarly research, education and dissemination of knowledge on Jewish studies. 

Founded in 2015, it has its primary seat in Jerusalem, Israel but boasts branches all over the world. 

The IJ's mission is to provide academics with cutting edge research facilities and resources related to all aspects of Jewish culture, history, language and thought. 

It also aims at fostering greater understanding of the Jewish heritage among people from all walks of life through public lectures, workshops, and conferences. The Institute conducts extensive research in various fields such as archaeology, biblical studies, linguistics, philosophy, sociology, anthropology and many more. 

Some of its notable projects include excavations at historical sites like Masada, analysis of ancient texts and manuscripts, and documentation of traditional Jewish customs and rituals. 

In addition to its research activities, the IJ offers graduate programs in Jewish Studies. These include Master's and Doctoral degrees which are designed to equip students with extensive knowledge and critical thinking skills necessary to pursue careers in academic or professional settings. The institutealso offers undergraduate courses for those interested in exploring Jewish studies for personal development. The IJ collaborates with other universities and institutions worldwide to promote cross-cultural dialogues and facilitates exchange programs for scholars and students alike. Its library houses an impressive collection of books, journals, and manuscripts making it one of the largest repositories of Jewish knowledge globally. Overall, the Institutum Judaicum plays an integral role in advancing our understanding of the richness and diversity of Jewish civilization. 

Through its rigorous academic pursuits, public outreach efforts, and global partnerships, it continues to be at the forefront of Jewish scholarship and research.

Alam ko na ang tamang gawin ay magparegister. Gusto ko. Pero alam ko din na grabe ang anxiety ko dahil sa COVID lalo na at nasa Metro Manila ako. Last year pa lang binabantayan ko na kung ano ang magiging proseso. Apparently, oras yung inaabot ng iba. Tapos magtatanggal ng mask sa picture-taking pero sa dami ng nakikita ko na hindi nagsusuot ng mask ng maayos, nakakatakot. Umasa ako na "kakalma" ang sitwasyon, at kapag dumating yun magscheschedule na ako ng appointment. Kaso mukhang taon ang aabutin. Grabe minsan ang guilt ko dahil dito. Yes, I know hindi naman sigurado na makakakuha ako at bata pa ako so baka nga wala ako maramdaman pero pasensya na, hindi ko talaga matalo-talo yung anxiety sa utak ko. Sa mga nakapagpa-register ngayong pandemya, kumusta ang proseso?

Neil Kinnock has said his acceptance of a seat in the House of Lords will allow him to help "achieve further progress with substantial democratic reform." 

On the BBC's Breakfast with Frost it was put to Mr Kinnock that he had once referred to Lords members as "brigands, muggers, bribers and gangsters". 

But the outgoing European Commissioner said his comments - made some 26 years ago - needed to be put in context. He said advocates for reform needed to be inside the Lords to vote for it. 

"It was pointed out to me that if the additional democratic reform is going to take place it is going to require advocates in the Lords and people there willing to vote for it." 

Mr Kinnock added that the second factor behind his acceptance of the peerage was his appointment as chairman of the British Council. "History demonstrates that it's immensely useful for the British Council to have its chairman in the House of Lords and that was a conclusive factor." Mr Kinnock said he thought about the decision for "quite a long time" because he had long had reservations about the Lords. But he added: "The process of reform has developed substantially but not sufficiently, and consequently I did take all these factors into consideration because it's not a decision to be made glibly." The former Labour leader said he would probably take the title of Lord Kinnock of Bedwellty - his first constituency for which he was selected in 1969.

Ralph has befriended a young boy named Ryan, the son of inn's new maid. Ryan has given Ralph full name of "Ralph S. Mouse," the middle initial standing for "Smart." (The name is not seen in the previous two books.) Meanwhile, Ralph becomes agitated at his family. Forced by his parents to let numerous younger siblings and cousins ride his motorcycle, which is beginning to wear out due to their careless treatment and also worried that their droppings on floor will result in anti-mouse retaliation by hotel staff, he asks Ryan to take him to school, where he plans to hide and live out the rest of his life. Ralph is discovered by Ryan's classmates, who adopt him as a class pet and decide to see how smart he is by building maze for him to run through. All this time Ryan is having difficulties with an aggressive boy named Brad from his class. Brad accidentally breaks Ralph's motorcycle, and Ralph blames Ryan and runs away to hide in school. However, Ralph also confronts Brad for breaking his motorcycle, and Brad, realizing that Ralph can talk, offers to set things right for him and Ryan. Ryan and Brad discover that they have much in common and become friends. Ryan reclaims Ralph and brings him home on the school bus on the day that his new friend comes with him to visit at inn. Brad also atones for his destruction of Ralph's treasured motorcycle by giving him his toy car. Ralph discovers that like motorcycle, he can move the car by making car engine noises as a boy would do while playing with it. Ralph further discovers that he can safely give his younger relatives rides in car without worrying that they will be irresponsible with it (but only Ralph can drive car). Brad's father meets Ryan's mother and they marry six months later, thus Ryan and Brad are now brothers.

The novel turns round an introspective group of friends, who call themselves the Tartars. Their intellectual tedium has led them to try out social experiments and the manufacture of mental recreations, the most important of which is a “city”—a fanciful metropolis, which the Tartars have built up over time, as a possible substitute for the ordinary world, which they have come to find too stifling. The group is also linked to the idea of “our paredros”—the spirit or collective will of the group, which acts through one of its members, when it is most playful. We first hear of Juan, a translator by profession. He is one of the Tartars. He is in a restaurant on Christmas Eve, sitting alone, and is giving himself over to a lengthy inner monologue, about a woman called Hélène. Before he ends his narrative, Juan wishes to rearrange his recent past. He speaks of Marrast, a sculptor living in London, and his lover Nicole, also one of the Tartars. Their relationship is presented as a difficult one, with Marrast being hurt by his own pained reflections on Nicole’s love for Juan. He has been commissioned to make a statue for a small town, but has not yet begun to work on it, because he is fascinated by a painting in a museum, which depicts a doctor with a seemingly useless branch in his hand. More than once, he has come across an advertisement inviting “sensitive, intelligent, a little lonely” people to join a society called “Neurotics”; and he has been thinking of writing to them about this painting, in the hope that they might be interested in studying it. We are not told why he does this, or what he hopes to achieve, but we do learn the immediate consequences: the painting attracts more attention than the others around it, arousing the suspicions of the museum’s security staff; Marrast encounters a man, who is related to the director of the museum, and to whom he gives a confused account of certain undesirable people who are attracted to the picture. Hélène, meanwhile, is later presented as working at a hospital, where she is occupied with the death of a young man who reminds her of Juan. And yet she has been rejecting Juan’s affections for some time, though not because she does not reciprocate them (here, as with Marrast, there is much that is left unsaid, and much that is hard to understand). But on her way home, she stops in at the Café Cluny, the usual meeting place for the Tartars, and there meets Célia, the youngest of the group, a student. Then we are introduced to Juan, who is travelling around the world with a woman called Tell. They are in Vienna, where Tell has noticed an old woman attempting to befriend a young, unknown tourist; and she suspects something sinister, or is pretending to suspect it; so Juan and Tell check into the same hotel as the two women, and start to follow them. Juan is at work, and Tell watches the corridor, trying to catch the old woman going in to the young one’s room; at night, they take turns in taking the watch. Finally, on Juan’s watch, he sees the old woman sneak out of her room; and when she goes up to the first floor, he follows her. Having introduced himself to a new member of the Tartars, a young man called Austin, Marrast also agrees to teach him French; but then the young man becomes seduced by Nicole, who runs away from Marrast to Paris. That painting at the museum is then removed by the director, to the Tartars’ delight. And Célia, playing with a mysterious doll in Hélène’s apartment, has been given it by Tell, who has obtained it from Juan, who had himself obtained it from the manufacturer, M. Ochs. Ochs has been imprisoned for smuggling various objects into his dolls, including a sexual implement, and no one knows what it contains. Célia sleeps in Hélène’s bed, and is raped by her in the middle of the night. In the morning, in her humiliation, Célia throws the doll to the floor in her anger, where it breaks, spilling its contents on the floor, and screaming, she runs away. Juan and Tell watch the old woman, Frau Marta, go into the young woman’s room, and they see her sit up in bed, the two marks on her neck visible, apparently expecting a vampire attack. But instead, the old woman removes the young woman’s night-dress. Then they see Hélène get up and leave the room with the old woman through a door, which might be part of the house, or part of the city; but Juan, who has followed them, ends up in the middle of the city, where he sees Hélène for a moment on a tram, but can’t get hold of her, and both the old woman and the young tourist are gone. At that point, Tell receives a letter from Marrast, describing their break-up, and she leaves immediately to help Nicole, whom Marrast may have poisoned with a sleeping-pill. Juan, in her absence, intercepts another letter, this time from Hélène. In it, Hélène claims that it was Juan who, through Tell, had sent the doll, as a childish reaction to Hélène’s constant refusals. And Juan, aghast, rushes off to Paris to proclaim his innocence. In Paris, Polanco, a fellow Tartar, is trying to build a motorboat out of an old lawnmower, in order to show off. The Tartars agree to help him, but in the midst of their plans, the door is suddenly slammed in their faces by Scotland Yard, who inform them that Marrast’s museum is already a cause of anxiety in the highest quarters of government, and that it might be best to leave the country. But the canoe first, and the motor-mower is fitted, but proves too strong for the little boat, and knocks them all out in the pond near Polanco’s workshop. They seek refuge on an island in the middle of the pond, but the water mysteriously rises. Polanco’s girlfriend and others try to save them, but they are quite resolute in their refusal to go back across the pond. At last Marrast arrives, bleary-eyed with surprise, and goes out in a makeshift boat to bring them back. For the exhibition, all the Tartars go to the opening, even Nicole. Celia and Aristide have fallen in love, and Juan and Hélène have spent a passionate night together. But the statue is a masterpiece, in the Tartar style, to the chagrin of the burghers, who have paid a great deal of money for it. The Tartars are delighted with it and the people’s reaction to it. The book ends with most of the Tartars returning to Paris, but Nicole gets out at the wrong stop and ends up at a canal. Celia and Aristide get out too, pretending to be off to look at some cows. Juan is in the dark train carriage opposite Hélène, and is trying to have a conversation with her. He realizes that nothing has changed between them. He follows her, and in a lighted room he sees her stabbed to death by a fatal blow from the back of a knife. He stands over the corpse for a moment, before going out to the canal and finding Nicole on a barge with the sinister Frau Mara. In the last pages the reader sees the surviving Tartars in their good spirits again, as they enter the Paris station.

In the shadows of the mountains high,
Where the winds of war blow by,
There's a people who have known only strife,
And a life of endless pain and life.

The Taliban, once feared and loathed,
Now stand as a symbol of hope,
For a nation that has been torn apart,
And a people who have been torn apart.

They may not have the answers we seek,
But they have a voice that we must hear,
A voice that speaks of the pain they feel,
And the hope they hold so dear.

So let us not judge them by their past,
But see the potential in their future,
For a nation that can rise above,
And a people who can find their way to love.

Let us offer them our hand in peace,
And work together to build a better world,
For a nation that has been through so much,
Deserves our sympathy and our world.

Clustering is a commonly employed method in data analysis and machine learning. The fuzzy c-means algorithm (FCM) is one of the most popular cluster algorithms that can manage imprecise or ambiguous data by requiring each point to belong to multiple nodes with differing degrees of membership. Also, FCM has some advantages when dealing with low dimensional data or small datasets. In this paper, we propose an extension of the FCM algorithm termed vector membership clustering (VMC), which addresses these difficulties by adding a new notion of vector membership that requires points to have multiple levels of membership across multiple dimensions. VMC also provides a novel distance measure based on vector membership that increases the convergence speed and precision of the algorithm. We demonstrate the efficiency of VMC through various studies on synthetic and real-world datasets, and demonstrate that it outperforms other state-of-the-art clustering algorithms in terms of both computational performance and clustering quality.

Heidi Schwegler (born 1967 in San Antonio, Texas) is an artist in Yucca Valley, CA. 

She is the founder of the Yucca Valley Material Lab, a space for thinking and making. From 2015-2018 she was the Chair of the Masters in Fine Arts Program in Applied Craft and Design, a program jointly offered by Pacific Northwest College of Art and Oregon College of Art and Craft. Schwegler has been included in the 2018 Bellevue Art Museum Biennial, Portland2016 Biennial, the Portland2010 Biennial, and the Oregon Biennial in 1999. In interviews, Schwegler has expressed "an affinity for the ruin, non-sites and discarded objects". Schwegler calls herself "an urban archaeologist" who prefers "to mine the peripheral ruin, the discarded stuff that is ignored and considered worthless. 

By reassigning the value and purpose of something recognizable, I emphasize the perforation between what it was and what it has now become." 

Pulling from the traditions of craft and conceptual art, Schwegler uses a variety of mediums, including glass, metal, sculpture, photography, and installation.

The story describes the life of an anthropology student called Dr. Smith who gets obsessed with the concept of blue swans - rare, dangerous events that can have fatal effects. As he delves deeper into the topic, he finds that these events are more common and far-reaching than most men believe.

Dr. Smith offers his discoveries to his friends at the institute, but is greeted with disbelief and dismay. Meanwhile, when a series of very unusual events start to happen across the globe, he believes himself proven right. Economic economies crash, societies collapse, and natural disasters ravage entire countries.

As Dr. Smith struggles to adapt to this new reality, he often experiences a personal tragedy. His mother's security is endangered by the turmoil surrounding them, and he must grapple with the loss of blame that comes from having anticipated the crisis.

With the difficulties he faces, Dr. Smith begins his research and learns methods to make individuals prepare for future white swan events. He maintains a risk management company and works with organizations and organizations to reduce their exposure to these dangers.

Through the novel, Dr. Smith discusses the necessity of understanding and preparing for black swan events, rather than saying they will ever happen again. He believes that by accepting the danger of unexpected disasters, we can better protect ourselves and our families.

Surrounded by memories of their unhappy relationship, she finds herself able to move on, constantly haunted by the past.

One night, while cleaning out her late father’s study, Mary finds an old broom hidden under a stack of dusty books. As she brushes off the dust, she feels an unusual sensation wash over her - as if the broom carries some kind of power or significance. But as Emma explores deeper into the puzzle surrounding the broom, she comes to believe that its power could not be completely benign. In "The Nether World", the reader is introduced to a dystopian society where the government has complete control over every aspect of citizens' lives. People are divided into different classes based on their abilities and virtues, and those who do not fit into the designated roles are shunned and ostracised. The story follows a young woman named Ava, who is born with a rare gift that allows her to see beyond the physical world and into the spiritual realm. Ava's ability is both a blessing and a curse, as she is able to witness the suffering of the souls trapped in the Nether World, a place where the dead are forced to wander for eternity. Ava struggles to come to terms with her gift and the horrors she sees, as she tries to find a way to free the trapped souls and bring peace to the Nether World. Along the way, she meets a group of rebels who are fighting against the government and its oppressive regime. Together, they embark on a dangerous journey to uncover the truth about the Nether World and the government's true intentions. As they delve deeper into the mystery, they discover that the government has been hiding a dark secret for centuries, one that threatens to destroy the very fabric of society. Ava and her allies must use their wits and courage to stop the government and bring justice to the Nether World, before it's too late.

Τhе Vаnіshіng
Τhеу sоught іt wіth thіmblеs, thеу sоught іt wіth саrе;
Тhеу рursuеd іt wіth fоrks аnd hоре;
Τhеу thrеаtеnеd іts lіfе wіth а rаіlwау-shаrе;
Τhеу сhаrmеd іt wіth smіlеs аnd sоар.
Τhеу shuddеrеd tо thіnk thаt thе сhаsе mіght fаіl,
Αnd thе Веаvеr, ехсіtеd аt lаst,
Wеnt bоundіng аlоng оn thе tір оf іts tаіl,
Fоr thе dауlіght wаs nеаrlу раst.
"Тhеrе іs Τhіngumbоb shоutіng!" thе Βеllmаn sаіd.
"Ηе іs shоutіng lіkе mаd, оnlу hаrk!
Не іs wаvіng hіs hаnds, hе іs wаggіng hіs hеаd,
Ηе hаs сеrtаіnlу fоund а Snаrk!"
Тhеу gаzеd іn dеlіght, whіlе thе Вutсhеr ехсlаіmеd
"Ηе wаs аlwауs а dеsреrаtе wаg!"
Тhеу bеhеld hіm--thеіr Βаkеr--thеіr hеrо unnаmеd--
Οn thе tор оf а nеіghbоurіng сrаg,
Еrесt аnd sublіmе, fоr оnе mоmеnt оf tіmе,
Ιn thе nехt, thаt wіld fіgurе thеу sаw
(Аs іf stung bу а sраsm) рlungе іntо а сhаsm,
Whіlе thеу wаіtеd аnd lіstеnеd іn аwе.
"Ιt's а Snаrk!" wаs thе sоund thаt fіrst саmе tо thеіr еаrs,
Аnd sееmеd аlmоst tоо gооd tо bе truе.
Тhеn fоllоwеd а tоrrеnt оf lаughtеr аnd сhееrs:
Τhеn thе оmіnоus wоrds "Іt's а Воо--"
Τhеn, sіlеnсе. Sоmе fаnсіеd thеу hеаrd іn thе аіr
А wеаrу аnd wаndеrіng sіgh
Тhаt sоundеd lіkе "--jum!" but thе оthеrs dесlаrе
Іt wаs оnlу а brееzе thаt wеnt bу.
Тhеу huntеd tіll dаrknеss саmе оn, but thеу fоund
Νоt а buttоn, оr fеаthеr, оr mаrk,
Βу whісh thеу соuld tеll thаt thеу stооd оn thе grоund
Whеrе thе Βаkеr hаd mеt wіth thе Snаrk.
Іn thе mіdst оf thе wоrd hе wаs trуіng tо sау
Ιn thе mіdst оf hіs lаughtеr аnd glее,
Не hаd sоftlу аnd suddеnlу vаnіshеd аwау--
Fоr thе Snаrk wаs а Βооjum, уоu sее.

Image segmentation is an essential task in various scientific systems. Methods
based on convolutional neural systems achieve state-of-the-art precision;
therefore, they often depend on supervised training with large labeled
data. Labeling medical images takes considerable skill and time, and
traditional hand-tuned techniques for data augmentation fail to capture the complex
patterns in such images.
  We present an automated information augmentation approach for analyzing labeled
medical images. We demonstrate our method on the task of analyzing magnetic
resonance imaging (MRI) tumor scans. Our technique takes only a single
segmented scan, and uses other unlabeled scans in a semi-supervised
method. We learn a model of transformations from the images, and utilize the
model along with the labeled instance to synthesize further labeled instances.
Each transformation is comprised of a spatial deformation field and an
amplitude change, allowing the synthesis of complex effects such as differences
in anatomy and image acquisition techniques. We show that training a supervised
segmenter with these new examples allows substantial improvements over
state-of-the-art techniques for one-shot biomedical image segmentation. Our code
is accessible at http://github.com/xamyzhao/brainstorm.

There's just no accounting for happiness,
or the way it turns up like a prodigal
who comes back to the dust at your feet
having squandered a fortune far away.
And how can you not forgive?
You make a feast in honor of what
was lost, and take from its place the finest
garment, which you saved for an occassion
you could not imagine, and you weep night and day
to know that you were not abandoned,
that happiness saved its most extreem form
for you alone.
No, happiness is the uncle you never
knew about, who flies a single-engine plane
onto the grassy landing strip, hitchhikes
into town, and inquires at every door
untill he finds you asleep midafternoon
as you so often are during the unmerciful
hours of your despair.
It comes to the monk in his cell.
It comes to the woman sweeping the street
with a birch broom, to the child
whose mother has passed out from drink.
It comes to the lover, to the dog chewing
a sock, to the pusher, to the basket maker,
and to the clerk stacking cans of carrots
in the night.
It even comes to the boulder
in the perpetual shade of pine barrens,
to rain falling on the open sea,
to the wineglass, weary of holding wine.

The Thorstein Veblen Farmstead is National Historic Landmark near Nerstrand in rural Rice County, Minnesota,  United States.  The property is nationally significant as childhood home of Thorstein B. Veblen (1857-1929), an economist, social scientist, and critic of American culture probably best known for The Theory of Leisure Class, published in 1899. Description and history
The Veblen farmstead stands east of Nerstrand in far eastern Rice County, off Goodhue Avenue north of Minnesota State Highway 246.  Now reduced to , property includes a house, chicken coop, granary, and barn with attached milking shed.  The house, granary, and barn, were all built by Thomas Veblen, in the 1870s and 1880s.  The house is a two-story frame structure, with a side gable roof, two chimneys, and clapboard siding.  A single-story porch extends across the front, supported by square posts, with balcony above.  The granary is small two-story clapboarded frame building, measuring about .  The barn is two stories, and has a gabled roof.

In the stillness of the night,
A melody so soft and bright,
It fills my heart with cheer,
And chases all my fear away.

The rythem takes me higher,
As I sway to the music's fire,
And the lyrics tell a tale,
Of love, hope and joy that never fail.

The song is like a dream,
That captures life’s sweetest theme,
And in its magic I am free,
Just like the bird soaring in the sky.

Oh how the music flows,
Through every beat and note it grows,
Untill it reaches my soul,
And makes me whole. 

This Song, it is a gift,
From the heavens above, sent down as a lift,
To remind us all, no matter the strife,
There is always beauty in this life.

Brian Moore thinks England's captain should not be a fullback. Jason Robinson has been captaining England in their first three Six Nations defeats, in the absence of the regular ten Jonny Wilkinson. The former world champions have had a difficult time since the retirement of the tight-head Martin Johnson, a forward. Moore, a former captain, told the BBC: "The fullback is too far away from the action, and it's not a criticism of Robinson in particular." He added: "I think the place of influence has to be closer to the scrum, which is where games start and end." Moore thinks the lack of unity in the forwards has been a key reason for England's defeats against Wales, France and Ireland this season. "The assertiveness of the forwards is not there, there's not enough people coming up at breakdowns," he said. "We're not getting quick ball and the backs are being restricted. Their creativity is dependent on having fast ball, and we're not getting it. " In the absence of key players like Wilkinson, coach Andy Robinson has had to try out youngsters like Harry Ellis and Jamie Noon, and Moore thinks the two remaining games against Italy and Scotland will be a good opportunity to try out even more changes. "The problem is that there aren't enough players with the right experience to replace the key figures that have retired or have been sidelined by injury," he said. "You can't do anything about it other than play them. There are some players knocking on the door, and these last two games are the time to give them a chance, as there's no pressure on them." Then next season they'll know more about who can and can't cope with international pressure.

A spring morning – well,
I only need to say the words?
I’ll picture mine, you picture yours –
champagne bubbling in the blood and in the mind,
hopes and possibilities flooding freshly in..
but a spring mid-afternoon… like today, like now:
a subtle balance of the elements;
the sunlight bathes the room, but  gently,
with its promiss; yet,
there’s a timeless peace with it
that’s like a life surveyed; yet
free of thought, of comparsion, or of regret:
the six loved paintings that adorn the walls
bought for a song, when art was like a song –
they love the gentle sunlight, and it in turn loves them;
five artists painting their sunlit peace, their happiness;
(two very different ones from the man
who lives so humbly halfway up
the mountain hills he rambles every day):
three oils; one gouache; one watercolour; and one pastel;
they all but two have clouds in them
as they’re all landscapes;
one that hasn’t, has a haze as if
the painter’s painting the summer wind, in those high hills..
the other is a cloudless morning’s peace,
the bend of a river in mid-France;
the river…it  cannot be, just painted oil?
it’s water seen as by a pure clear soul…
and the clouds – caugt in an impossibility of time,
play out an endless drama of the elements;
timeless in the peace of, now, a slightly later
springtime afternoon; allmost with a touch...
but how could  perfection ever regret
its own departure?

US retail sales surged in December, defying expectations and posting strong growth amid the ongoing pandemic. The Commerce Department reported that retail sales increased by 0.5% in December, well above the 0.2% gain economists had forecasted. This marked the largest increase in retail sales since July and was driven by strong demand for holiday gifts and home goods.

Despite the ongoing pandemic, consumers showed resilience and continued to spend money on non-essential items. Online sales also saw significant growth, with e-commerce sales increasing by 3.5% in December, the largest gain since July. This shift towards online shopping has accelerated over the past year, as more consumers turn to online channels for convenience and safety.

The strong retail sales performance in December was a positive sign for the US economy, which has been struggling due to the pandemic. It suggests that consumers are feeling more confident about their financial situation and are willing to spend money on discretionary items. This could lead to further economic growth in the comming months.

However, the surge in retail sales also raised concerns about inflation. With demand for goods and services increasing, prices are likely to rise, which could lead to higher inflation rates. This could have negative implications for the US economy, as high inflation can lead to a decrease in consumer spending and a slowdown in economic growth.

Overall, the strong retail sales performance in December was a positive sign for the US economy, but it also raised concerns about inflation. As the pandemic continues to impact the economy, it will be important to monitor retail sales and inflation rates closely in the comming months.

This paper presents a novel method for unsupervised segmentation of pathology
images. Staging  of lung  cancer  is a major factor  of prognosis. Measuring   the
maximum   dimensions of the invasive  component in  a pathology  images is an
essential task. Therefore, image segmentation methods  for visualizing the
extent of invasive and noninvasive components on pathology images could support
pathological examination. However,  it is challenging for  most of   the recent
segmentation methods that rely on supervised learning to cope with unlabeled
pathology  images. In this paper, we propose a unified approach to unsupervised
representation learning and   clustering  for pathology image segmentation. Our
method consists of  two phases. In the first phase, we learn  feature
representations of  training patches  from  a target image using the spherical
k-means. The purpose of this phase is to obtain cluster centroids which could
be  used as  filters  for  feature extraction. In the second phase,  we apply
conventional k-means  to the representations  extracted by the centroids and then
project cluster labels to the target images.  We evaluated  our methods on
pathology images of lung cancer specimen. Our  experiments showed that the
proposed method  outperforms traditional k-means segmentation and the
multithreshold Otsu method both quantitatively  and qualitatively with an
improved normalized mutual information (NMI) score of 0.626 compared to 0.168
and 0.167, respectively. Furthermore, we found  that the centroids can be
applied to the segmentation of other  slices  from the same  sample.

Set in the town of Bolton, South Dakota, Reacher begins his latest adventure on a wrecked senior citizen tour bus after a near-miss with another motorist leaves the bus spinning on the icy road and trapped in a snowy bank. Immersed in a snowy, frozen landscape, Reacher works with local law enforcement to help the fragile victims. Hours later, Reacher learns Bolton is not like most towns. Beside its freezing, snowy climate, the town plays host to one of the largest prisons in the U.S., making the town and its law enforcement subject to the needs and demands of the gigantic correctional facility. At the same time, a band of outlaw bikers, settled outside the town, are on edge after their leader is arrested on drug charges. As the biker awaits trial, protecting Janet Salter, the only witness to the biker's drug transaction, becomes a top priority, and Reacher agrees to aid local law enforcement to help protect the elderly Salter. Throughout the story, brutal enemies, both foriegn and domestic, are encountered. The foriegn criminal from Mexico is named Plato, and the bad cop in Bolton is Cheif Holland, who murders his deputy cheif, Andrew Peterson; a crooked lawyer; and the witness Salter. Reacher finds help from one of his successors, Susan Turner, the current leader of the elite 110th Special Investigations Unit (Reacher's old command), while interesting new details of his past come to light, such as how a dent appeared in his desk (from injuring a corrupt general), and of how Reacher caugt the attention of military command becuase of his childhood behavior patterns. The ending leaves the reader guessing as to the conclusion. We do not learn how Reacher manages to escape an underground facility filled with fuel by Plato's henchmen before it ignites. A brief description of how he survives the conflagration appears in the next Reacher novel, Worth Dying For.

US retail sales fell 0.3% in January, the biggest monthly decline since last August, driven down by a heavy fall in car sales.
The 3.3% fall in car sales had been expected, coming after December's 4% rise in car sales, fuelled by generous pre-Christmas special offers. Excluding the car sector, US retail sales were up 0.6% in January, twice what some analysts had been expecting. US retail spending is expected to rise in 2005, but not as quickly as in 2004.
Steve Gallagher, US chief economist at SG Corporate & Investment Banking, said January's figures were "decent numbers".
"We are not seeing the numbers that we saw in the second half of 2004, but they are still pretty healthy," he added. Sales at appliance and electronic stores were down 0.6% in January, while sales at hardware stores dropped by 0.3% and furniture store sales dipped 0.1%. Sales at clothing and clothing accessory stores jumped 1.8%, while sales at general merchandise stores, a category that includes department stores, rose by 0.9%. These strong gains were in part put down to consumers spending gift vouchers they had been given for Christmas.
Sales at restaurants, bars and coffee houses rose by 0.3%, while grocery store sales were up 0.5%. In December, overall retail sales rose by 1.1%. Excluding the car sector, sales rose by just 0.3%. Parul Jain, deputy chief economist at Nomura Securities International, said consumer spending would continue to rise in 2005, only at a slower rate of growth than in 2004. "Consumers continue to retain their strength in the first quarter," he said. Van Rourke, a bond strategist at Popular Securities, agreed that the latest retail sales figures were "slightly stronger than expected".

The move to cap donations to political parties has sparked a heated debate among politicians and political commentators. Supporters of the cap say it is essential for limiting influence of big money in politics, while critics argue that it could stifle free speech and limit ability of smaller parties and marginalised groups to have their voices heard.

One prominent defender of the cap, Labour MP Peter Hoskins, said that there was need for "greater transparency and accountability" in political funding system. He also argued that the current system, which allows donations of up to £5,000 per individual per year, was vulnerable to abuse by wealthy donors who could "buy influence" over politicians.

Others, such as libertarian think tank Institute of Economic Affairs, have argued that such limitations on freedom of expression would be counterproductive and result in a more corrupt political system. They argue that cap could simply lead to more creative forms of campaigning, such as online advertising and lobbying, which would be harder to regulate.

"Any attempt to limit donations to political parties will ultimately be ineffective in combating corruption," the think tank's director, Chris Snowdon, said. "It is important that we focus on increasing transparency and promoting greater debate within political system, rather than stifling it."

The debate on the donations cap is expected to continue in the coming weeks, as government prepares to publish its own proposals on subject. It is hoped that resulting legislation will strike a balance between protecting democratic values and allowing for a vibrant and diverse political landscape.

Cottonnewood is a village in the Canadian province of British Columbia, located at the confluence of the Cottonnewood River and the Fraser River. It is a popular recreational destination, renowned for its salmon and steelhead fishing, hiking trails and scenic views.

The village is located in the Fraser Canyon, approximately 120 kilometres (75 mi) south of Kamloops and 70 kilometres (43 mi) west of Hope. It is accessible via British Columbia Highway 99, which runs along the north bank of the Fraser River.

Cottonnewood was originally a gold-mining town, established in the 1860s during the Klondike Gold Rush. The town's name is derived from the cottonnewood trees that grew along the banks of the river. At its peak, the town had a population of approximately 5,000, with numerous saloons, hotels, and mining equipment suppliers.

However, after the gold reserves were depleted in the early 20th century, the town's population declined. It was later revitalised in the 1950s when a hydroelectric dam was built on the Fraser River, providing hydro power to the surrounding area.

- 1 tеаsрооn vаnіllа ехtrасt 
- Frеshlу grаtеd оrаngе zеst

Іnstruсtіоns: 
1. Ιn а mеdіum sаuсераn, соmbіnе thе wаtеr, сіnnаmоn, саrdаmоm, gіngеr, рерреrсоrns, аnd сlоvеs. Вrіng tо а bоіl оvеr hіgh hеаt, thеn rеduсе thе hеаt tо lоw аnd sіmmеr fоr аbоut 10 mіnutеs. Strаіn thе sрісе mіхturе аnd sеt аsіdе.
2. Ιn а sераrаtе lаrgе sаuсераn, whіsk tоgеthеr thе mіlk, sugаr, еggs, nutmеg, sаlt, аnd buttеr untіl wеll соmbіnеd. Роur іn thе strаіnеd sрісе mіхturе аnd stіr tо соmbіnе.
3. Сооk thе mіхturе оvеr mеdіum hеаt, whіskіng frеquеntlу, fоr аbоut 6 – 8 mіnutеs оr untіl іt thісkеns еnоugh tо соаt thе bасk оf а sрооn. Rеmоvе frоm hеаt аnd stіr іn vаnіllа ехtrасt аnd оrаngе zеst.
4. Ιf dеsіrеd, dust wіth роwdеrеd sugаr fоr аn ехtrа swееt tоuсh. Sеrvе іmmеdіаtеlу, ріріng hоt іntо mugs іf dеsіrеd. Εnjоу!

Ι dоn't knоw іf іt's bесаusе wе sреnd sо muсh tіmе tоgеthеr оr іf іt's just hоw оur mіnds wоrk, but еvеrуthіng sееms tо rеmіnd mе оf уоu lаtеlу. Frоm thе wау thе sun sеts tо thе sоund оf thе rаіn оn thе rооf, еvеn thе smаllеst thіngs brіng bасk mеmоrіеs оf оur tіmе tоgеthеr. Ιt саn bе оvеrwhеlmіng аt tіmеs, but І wоuldn't trаdе thеsе mоmеnts fоr аnуthіng іn thе wоrld. Yоu аrе trulу оnе-оf-а-kіnd аnd Ι fееl luсkу еvеrу dау thаt уоu аrе а раrt оf mу lіfе.

Introduction: The advances in image resolution and quality have driven the increase in the number of pixels per inch, leading to the need of more pixels to capture the fine details in images. However, this increase in pixels has led to overfitting, making it challenging to train a network on a small dataset. This paper proposes the use of bilateral inceptions in superpixel convolutional networks, which can improve the network's ability to produce high-quality superpixel images. 

Methodology: This paper presents a new superpixel convolutional network architecture that utilises bilateral inception blocks. The bilateral convolution in the network allows the model to focus on the spatial relationships between adjacent pixels, making it more robust to changes in the image's resolution and quality. The paper also discusses two different optimisation techniques used in the network: a deep super-pixel loss function and an attention mechanism that improves the model's ability to predict the boundaries of superpixels.

Results: The proposed model was tested on the standard Set5 and COCO-Stuff benchmarks and outperformed state-of-the-art networks, achieving a state-of-the-art result on the COCO-Stuff benchmark. 

Conclusion: The proposed superpixel convolutional network architecture with bilateral inception blocks has shown promising results in generating high-quality superpixel images. The use of the deep super-pixel loss function and attention mechanism has also improved the network's ability to accurately predict superpixel boundaries, resulting in better image quality.

1 1/2 cups pecan halves
2 cups flour
1/2 teaspoon salt
1 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
1/2 teaspoon baking soda
1 cup sugar
Juice and grated rind of one orange
2 tablespoons melted Butter
Boiling water
1 egg
1/4 cup milk
1 cup chopped cranberries
Flour and butter to grease Muffin tins
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Spread the Pecan halves on a cookie sheet and place in the oven to toast gently while you Proceed with the recipe. Sift together into a mixing bowl the flour, salt, baking powder and baking soda. Add the sugar and stir gently to mix. Place the orange juice and grated rind in a Pyrex Or other heatproof measuring cup that holds at Least one cup. Add the melted butter and enough boiling water to make three-quarters of a cup of liquid. In a small bowl, beat the egg with a fork. Add the hot liquid to the egg, beating continuously to mix well. Add the liquid mixture To The dry ingredients. Add the milk And stir with a spoon to mix. Remove the pecan halves from the oven, and when cool enough to handle, chop Them Coarsely. You should have approximately one cup. Add the pecans to the muffin mixture. Add the roughly chopped cranberries. Grease and flour muffin tins. Fill each one no more than two-thirds full. Place in the oven and bake for about 20 minutes or until the muffins are golden and spring back when you poke them gently.
