Many machine learning tasks can be formulated in terms of predicting
structured outputs. In frameworks such as the structured support vector machine
(SVM-Struct) and the structured perceptron, discriminative functions are
learned by iteratively applying efficient maximum a posteriori (MAP) decoding.
However, maximum likelihood estimation (MLE) of probabilistic models over these
same structured spaces requires computing partition functions, which is
generally intractable. This paper presents a method for learning discrete
exponential family models using the Bethe approximation to the MLE. Remarkably,
this problem also reduces to iterative (MAP) decoding. This connection emerges
by combining the Bethe approximation with a Frank-Wolfe (FW) algorithm on a
convex dual objective which circumvents the intractable partition function. The
result is a new single loop algorithm MLE-Struct, which is substantially more
efficient than previous double-loop methods for approximate maximum likelihood
estimation. Our algorithm outperforms existing methods in experiments involving
image segmentation, matching problems from vision, and a new dataset of
university roommate assignments.

Title: Theoretical Analysis of Active Contours on Graphs

Abstract: Active Contours are a popular method used in computer vision and image processing for object segmentation and deformation. The traditional implementation of Active Contours on Euclidean spaces has been extended to handle graph structures, i.e., non-Euclidean spaces, in subsequent times. There are many variants of the Active Contour algorithm that work with graphs, including Geodesic Active Contours and Advanced Graph Cutting. This paper gives a mathematical study of these methods. While Geodesic Active Contours preserve the geodesic distance between adjacent nodes on the diagram, Advanced Graph Cutting gives a global minimum of a weighted cut function. Also, we review other experimental findings related to graph-structured Active Contours, including convergence results and performance data, among others. Overall, this journal contains a systematic review of the computational study of Active Contours on graphs, paving the way for further study and development in this topic.

1 pie crust, homemade or store-bought
4-5 pounds firm ripe plums (20-25 plums), halved, pitted (with skin)
1 1/2 cups plus 2 tablespoons sugar
2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice
1 vanilla bean, split lengthwise
8 ounces mascarpone
1/3 cup creme fraiche
2 tablespoons honey
Whipped cream
A 9" pie dish
Preheat oven to 350°F. 

Line pie dish with crust; crimp edges. Fully bake pie crust according to recipe or box instructions. 

Place plums in a large bowl; add 1 1/2 cups sugar and lemon juice. Scrape in seeds from half of vanilla bean; toss to coat. Divide plum mixture between two 13x9x2" glass baking dishes, arranging plums cut side down and overlapping slightly. Roast until juices are bubbling and slightly thickened and plums are tender but not falling apart, 40-60 minutes (cooking time will depend on ripeness of plums). Let cool slightly. Using a slotted spatula, transfer plums to a rimmed baking sheet. Cover loosely with plastic wrap; chill. 

Pour juices in baking dishes into a small saucepan. Bring to a boil and simmer until thickened and reduced to a scant 1/2 cup, 4-5 minutes; set glaze aside. 

Combine remaining 2 tablespoons sugar, mascarpone, creme fraiche, and honey in a medium bowl. Scrape in seeds from remaining half vanilla bean. 

Using an electric mixer, beat on high speed until mixture holds firm peaks (do not overbeat or mascarpone may curdle). 

DO AHEAD: Spread mascarpone cream evenly over bottom of crust. 

Arrange some chilled plum halves tightly (but not overlapping) in a single layer over mascarpone mixture. Starting at edges of pie crust, arrange remaining plum halves on top of base layer, overlapping tightly and forming a spiral to cover. Pie should dome slightly in the center. 

Using a pastry brush, spread some of glaze over plums (if glaze has firmed up, gently reheat, adding 1 tablespoon water and whisking to blend). 

Cut pie into slices. 

Top with whipped cream and drizzle with more plum syrup.

I am a clinical supervisor at an interdisciplinary clinic in a medium-sized city. The ongoing crisis has really brought to light so many systemic problems in the field of mental health in the U.S. It has also revealed so many problems in my own agency, and it makes it really hard to do my job and work where I do. I get no support from above, and my counselors are worn out, burned out, and I have no power to make things better. It has been weighing on me terribly, and at the same time I feel I don’t have the energy to look for something else. By the time I get home and walk my two dogs, I’m ready to go to bed. This is not the way I want to live, and I don’t know how to change it.

I'm feeling lost and alone right now, like I don't know where to turn or who to talk to. It feels like everything is spiraling out of control and there's no way to stop it. I don't want to burden anyone with my problems, so I keep them bottled up inside. But it's getting harder and harder to carry this weight around all by myself. If you're willing to listen, I would really appreciate it. Thank you for taking the time to read this.

The earliest presence of Islam in Chad can be traced back to Uqba ibn Nafi, whose descendants can be found settled in the Lake Chad region to this day. By the time Arab migrants began arriving from the east in the fourteenth century in sizeable numbers, the creed was already well established. 

Islamization in Chad was gradual, the effect of the slow spread of Islamic civilization beyond its political frontiers. Among Chadian Muslims, 48% professed to be Sunni, 21% Shia,  23% just Muslim and 4% Other. 

Islam in Chad was not influenced much by the great mystical movements of the Islamic Middle Ages, nor the fundamentalist upheavals that affected other countries. 

Consistent contact with West African Muslim traders and pilgrims may be the reason Chadian Muslims identify with the Tijaniyya order. Similarly, in the mid-nineteenth century, the Sanusiyya brotherhood was founded in Libya, which benefited from economic and political influence in the Lake Chad Basin around 1900. An Islamic revival movement, feared by some French, led by Sanusi fanatics, Chadian adherents, limited to the Awlad Sulayman Arabs and the Toubou of eastern Tibesti, have never been numerous.

It is possible to house, feed and dress the tens of thousands of homeless refugees in the Darfur region in Sudan with the help of modern map technology. A European consortium of companies and universities known as RESPOND is working on precise and up-to-date maps to ease the huge transport difficulties in providing aid to those in need. RESPOND is using satellite images to produce accurate maps that can be used on the spot. “RESPOND has produced everything from detailed maps of the road systems, rivers and villages to very general maps useful for very general planning purposes,” says Einar Bjrgo from Unosat, the United Nations organisation responsible for satellite mapping and also part of the RESPOND consortium. The group is using satellites from NASA, the European Space Agency and the International Earth Observation System. The satellites send down to earth stations which send the information on to RESPOND and other companies that are specialists in interpreting the data. “First the data is converted into images and then the map is translated into a map of the situation, damage or crisis,” says Stefan Voigt, who works in the remote sensing department at the German Aerospace Centre. This type of detailed analysis would usually take months but RESPOND can produce maps in less than 12 hours. “Our customers are generally not very good at interpreting satellite images and maps so it is our task to translate the data into information which is easy to understand and quickly usable,” says Voigt. The maps are distributed to aid agencies via the Internet and on CD-Roms. But the best map is the one you can hold in your hand, particularly in the remote areas where the Internet connections and computers are rare. “A map is a working document,” explains Herbert Hansen from the Belgian company RESPOND partner, Keyobs. “It is used, annotated, checked and so on, and that is why paper is needed.” “We print out the maps, laminate them if necessary and then put them in sealed plastic bags so that they can be used in the rain and in the shower.” The maps have been invaluable for humanitarian workers in Darfur, particularly during the rainy season when the normally dry riverbeds become flooded. According to Stephen Candillon, a RESPOND partner from the company Sertit, the floods are usually not deep but they have greatly reduced the transport capacity and ability of humanitarian organisations to work on the ground. With RESPOND’s rapid images, the aid organisations have been able to find alternative routes and can mark on the map which roads have been flooded and when. The aid agencies say that the combination of satellite images and the information collected by people on the ground has been extremely useful in ensuring that aid reaches those in need. The technology correspondent for The World programme, a co-production of the BBC World Service and WGBH in Boston.

Bifocal filters are very widely used due to their edge-preserving property. Usually, the operator chooses a filtering form, which is usually a Gaussian filter. In this paper, we will generalize the filtering form and in particular, deduce a gradient descent method to learn the filtering parameters from data. The learning process also applies to high-dimensional linear filters that operate in sparse feature spaces. For efficient filtering, we will use the permutohedral lattice construction. The learning of more general high-dimensional filters can be used in many diverse applications. First, we show the implementation in the applications where only a single filter is required for efficiency reasons. Then, we show how this learning process can be used to learn the pairwise potentials in densely connected conditional random fields and apply them to various image segmentation tasks. Finally, we introduce the filtering layers into convolutional neural networks and propose two-layered neural networks for the use of high-dimensional sparse data. This provides a new way to encode model structure in network architectures. A wide range of experiments confirms the use of the generalized filters.

Today the alarm clock failed to ring, today, 'Hooray,' the clock did not sing, today the trains made me late for work, I stared at the closed gates, today I stank to high heaven, no shower today The sad news sucked all my will out of me today I read the paper without seeing, Wondering what fuss about sleeping with the boss It was a whole page and there was a loss today I listened to sad love songs, I wondered what I had done wrong, The next time I will be strong Today I listened to sad love songs, I wondered what I had done wrong, a tragic life story I was not strong to bear today, today I thought of a future I longed for, I am a lady, a real woman, a positive woman! Today I sung, spoke and smiled, while having my nails done. Today I met life's challenge, Today I swung my fist in the air and I laughed with glee, Today I had a sweet revenge, I went to bed with a smile. Today, today is always happy! Today is always happy!  Sylvia Chidi, 2006, All rights reserved.

Jolanda Ceplak has urged Britain's Kelly Holmes to continue competing at the major championships.
Double Olympic gold medallist Holmes has strongly hinted she Will not run in this year's Worlds and is undecided about next month's European Indoors. But World Indoor 800m record holder Ceplak said: "There is never an easy race when she is in The field. There Is only excitement at what might Happen. "It is good For the sport. She always fetches the best out of everyone." Ceplak has Been a great Rival of Holmes' during the Briton's career and the pair fell out when Holmes questioned the manner of the Slovenian's runaway 800m victory at the 2002 European Championships. But the Controversy has since been forgotten, with Ceplak acting as pacemaker for Holmes' failed attempt on the British Indoor 1500m record at the Norwich Union Grand Prix in birmingham in 2003.
Ceplak added: "I like running against her - you know the race is always going to be fast. "That is the sort of competition that I like. She is special to me. She was like my idol from The beginning of my career." Meanwhile, Ceplak will be looking to follow up last saturday's win in Boston with a Fast time and victory in Friday's Night of Athletics in Erfurt, Germany. Britain's Jason Gardener had been expected to defend his 60m title in Erfurt but instead he will save himself for a competition in Leipzig on Sunday. Gardener's decision means Scotland's 400m man Ian Mackie will carry british hopes in what looks sure to be a tough preparation for next weekend's Norwich Union European trials in Sheffield.

Gambling is hugely popular, especially with tech-savvy criminals. 

Many extortionists are targeting net-based betting firms and threatening to cripple their websites with deluges of data unless a ransom is paid. 

But now deep defences are being put in place by some of the UK's biggest net firms to stop these attacks. 

Increasing numbers of attacks and the huge amounts of data being used to try to bump a site off the web are prompting firms to adopt the measures. 

"Net firms are realising that it's not just about anti-virus and firewalls," said Paul King, chief security architect at Cisco. "There are more things that can be done in the network to protect data centres." 

Mr King said the only way to properly combat these so-called Distributed Denial-of-Service attacks was with intelligent net-based systems. Many of the gambling sites suffering DDoS attacks are in offshore data and hosting centres, so any large scale data flood could knock out access to many more sites than just the one the criminals were targeting, said Mr King. 

This overspill effect was only likely to grow as attacks grow in size and scale. 

Malcolm Seagrave, security expert at Energis, said the most common types of attacks hit sites with 10 megabytes of data over short periods of time. Bigger attacks sending down 200 megabytes of traffic or more were rarely seen, he said. "It does feel like they are turning the dial because you see this traffic gradually growing," he said. 

So far there have been no attacks involving gigabytes of data, said Mr Seagrave. However, he added that it was only a matter of time before such large attacks were mounted. 

Maria Capella, spokeswoman for net provider Pipex, said that when DDoS attacks were at their height, customers were getting hit every four to five days. 

The defences being put in place constantly monitor the streams of data flowing across networks and pluck out the traffic destined for target sites. "It's about understanding what's genuine traffic and keeping attack traffic from going to the site," she said. 

"We study the profile of their traffic and as soon as we see an anomaly in the profile that's when we start to get the backbone engineering boys to see if we are going to sustain an attack," said Ms Capella. This traffic can be hard to spot because DDoS attacks typically use thousands of computers in many different countries, each participating machine only sends a small part of the entire data flood. 

Typically these computers have been infected by a virus or worm which reports its success and the net address of compromised machines back to the malicious hacker or hi-tech criminal that set off the virus. Hijacked computers are known as zombies or 'bots and collections of them are called 'bot nets. Many spammers rent out 'bot nets to help them anonymously send junk mail. Most of the zombies are based outside the country that hosts the target site so getting the attacking PCs shut off can be difficult. Often Pipex and other net suppliers do get advance notice that an attack is about to happen. "The serious players tend to precede an attack with some kind of ransom e-mail," said Ms Capella. "We ask, as part of the service we provide, that customers notify us of anything they have in advance that would give us forewarning." 

Once an attack is spotted dedicated net hardware takes over to remove the attack traffic and ensure that sites stay up. Energis took a similar approach, said Mr Seagrave. 

"We have technology out there that allows us to detect attacks in minutes rather than let network engineers spend hours pulling the information together," said Mr Seagrave. 

Also net firms were starting to work more closely together on the problem of DDoS attacks and pool information about where they are coming from. 

Information gathered on attacks and where they originated has led to some arrests. 

He said Energis also did its own intelligence work to get in insight into which sites criminal gangs plan to target. "We have people in places where they shouldn't be, monitoring tech sites," he said. Sometimes though, he said, spotting the next victim was easy. "You can see them going alphabetically through the list with the gambling sites, trying one after another," said Mr Seagrave.

Billy Bathgate is a young boy living in a poverty-stricken neighbourhood of New York City during the 1930s. His life takes a turn when he befriends Dutch Schultz, a local gangster who offers to take him under his wing and show him how to make a living as a criminal.
At first, Billy is hesitant, but he begins to see the potential benefits of a life of crime. He learns how to pick locks and pull off small-time scams, and soon finds himself swept up in the world of organised crime.
However, with the help of a group of honest friends, including a young girl named Dee Beers and her father, a policeman, Billy realises that a life of crime isn't what he wants after all. He makes the tough decision to turn away from Dutch and leave the life behind.
In the end, Billy emerges as a hero for standing up against corruption and fighting for truth and justice. He goes on to lead a successful career as an author, chronicling his experiences growing up in the city and the lessons he learned about the dangers of organised crime.

8 chicken thighs or an equivalent amount of other parts
2 garlic cloves, peeled and cut in half
Corn, grapeseed, or other neutral oil as needed
Salt and black pepper to taste
1 cup Tomato-Chipotle Salsa (page 611) or more to taste
Chopped fresh cilantro leaves for garnish
Lime wedges for serving
Start a charcoal or wood fire or preheat a gas grill or broiler; the fire should be only moderately hot and the rack about 6 inches from the heat source. Rub the chicken with the cut side of the garlic cloves, brush on the oil, and season with salt and pepper. 

Grill carefully so the chicken doesnt burn, making sure the skin crisps and browns and the interior cooks through; it will take about 20 minutes. 

Brush the chicken heavily with the chipotle sauce on both sides and cook for just another minute or two. Serve, hot or at room temperature, garnished with cilantro and with the lime wedges on the side. Substitute about 1 pound boneless, skinless chicken or turkey breast, which will take less than 10 minutes to grill. 

When done, cut into chunks and toss with the chipotle sauce. Serve on toothpicks. 

Bone-in pork chops, preferably from the shoulder end, can be done exactly like the chicken in the main recipe; cooking time will be a little shorter (do not allow them to dry out). Boneless pork (like tenderloin) can be used as in the chicken breast variation.

C​h​e​r​i​e​ ​P​i​p​e​r​ ​(​b​o​r​n​ ​J​u​n​e​ ​2​9​,​ ​1​9​8​1​)​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​C​a​n​a​d​i​a​n​ ​f​o​r​m​e​r​ ​i​c​e​ ​h​o​c​k​e​y​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​ ​r​e​s​i​d​i​n​g​ ​i​n​ ​M​a​r​k​h​a​m​,​ ​O​n​t​a​r​i​o​.​ ​S​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​m​e​m​b​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​a​n​a​d​i​a​n​ ​n​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​w​o​m​e​n​'​s​ ​h​o​c​k​e​y​ ​t​e​a​m​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​l​a​y​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​B​r​a​m​p​t​o​n​ ​T​h​u​n​d​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​a​n​a​d​i​a​n​ ​W​o​m​e​n​'​s​ ​H​o​c​k​e​y​ ​L​e​a​g​u​e​ ​(​C​W​H​L​)​.​ ​ ​P​i​p​e​r​ ​h​a​s​ ​w​o​n​ ​t​h​r​e​e​ ​O​l​y​m​p​i​c​ ​g​o​l​d​ ​m​e​d​a​l​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​a​n​a​d​i​a​n​ ​n​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​t​e​a​m​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​2​,​ ​2​0​0​6​ ​a​n​d​ ​2​0​1​0​,​ ​a​s​ ​w​e​l​l​ ​a​s​ ​o​n​e​ ​w​o​r​l​d​ ​c​h​a​m​p​i​o​n​s​h​i​p​ ​t​i​t​l​e​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​4​.​ ​P​l​a​y​i​n​g​ ​c​a​r​e​e​r​
​S​h​e​ ​c​o​m​p​e​t​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​C​a​n​a​d​a​'​s​ ​U​n​d​e​r​ ​2​2​ ​t​e​a​m​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​1​9​9​9​ ​t​o​ ​2​0​0​1​.​ ​I​n​ ​1​9​9​9​,​ ​s​h​e​ ​c​o​m​p​e​t​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​O​n​t​a​r​i​o​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​a​n​a​d​a​ ​W​i​n​t​e​r​ ​G​a​m​e​s​.​ ​D​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​2​0​0​0​–​0​1​ ​N​W​H​L​ ​s​e​a​s​o​n​,​ ​C​h​e​r​i​e​ ​P​i​p​e​r​ ​p​l​a​y​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​B​e​a​t​r​i​c​e​ ​A​e​r​o​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​i​n​i​s​h​e​d​ ​s​e​v​e​n​t​h​ ​i​n​ ​l​e​a​g​u​e​ ​s​c​o​r​i​n​g​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​3​7​ ​p​o​i​n​t​s​.​ ​P​i​p​e​r​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​m​e​m​b​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​n​d​e​r​-​2​2​ ​t​e​a​m​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​2​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​s​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​n​a​m​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​O​l​y​m​p​i​c​ ​t​e​a​m​ ​f​o​r​ ​2​0​0​2​ ​S​a​l​t​ ​L​a​k​e​ ​C​i​t​y​ ​G​a​m​e​s​ ​a​h​e​a​d​ ​o​f​ ​v​e​t​e​r​a​n​ ​N​a​n​c​y​ ​D​r​o​l​e​t​ ​a​s​ ​p​a​r​t​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​m​o​v​e​ ​t​o​ ​s​h​a​k​e​ ​u​p​ ​a​ ​C​a​n​a​d​i​a​n​ ​t​e​a​m​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​h​a​d​ ​l​o​s​t​ ​e​i​g​h​t​ ​c​o​n​s​e​c​u​t​i​v​e​ ​g​a​m​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​n​i​t​e​d​ ​S​t​a​t​e​s​.​ ​I​t​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​d​e​c​i​s​i​o​n​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​s​h​o​c​k​e​d​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​m​e​m​b​e​r​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​e​a​m​.​ ​S​h​e​ ​r​e​c​o​r​d​e​d​ ​a​ ​g​o​a​l​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​n​ ​a​s​s​i​s​t​ ​i​n​ ​h​e​r​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​O​l​y​m​p​i​c​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​i​n​i​s​h​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​o​u​r​n​a​m​e​n​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​f​i​v​e​ ​p​o​i​n​t​s​ ​i​n​ ​f​i​v​e​ ​g​a​m​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​h​e​l​p​i​n​g​ ​C​a​n​a​d​a​ ​w​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​o​l​d​ ​m​e​d​a​l​.​

Cake Batter
1/2 cup butter, softened
1/4 cup sugar
3/4 cup honey
1/8 teaspoon salt
1/2 cup cocoa
1/3 cup cold water
2 1/2 cups sifted cake flour
1 cup cold water
1 teaspoon baking soda
2 tablespoons boiling water
3 egg whites
Caramel Pecan Topping
2/3 cup brown sugar, packed firmly
1 1/2 teaspoons flour
2 egg yolks
2/3 cup milk
1 tablespoon butter
1/2 cup pecans, chopped
Chocolate Glaze
1 (1 ounce) unsweetened chocolate square, chopped
1 cup sugar
1/4 cup butter
1/3 cup milk
1/8 teaspoon salt
1/2 teaspoon vanilla
3/4 cup sugar
Cake Batter:. In a large bowl, cream together butter, 1/4 cup sugar, honey, and salt until light and fluffy. In a small bowl, combine cocoa and 1/3 cup water. Add to the creamed mixture, blending well. Add cake flour alternately with 1 cup water; beat well after each addition. In a small dish, combine baking soda and 2 tablespoons water. Stir into the batter. In separate bowl, beat egg whites until frothy. Gradually beat in 3/4 cup sugar. Fold gently into cake batter. Pour batter into greased and floured 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan. Bake in preheated 350 degree oven about 50 minutes or until cake tests done. Remove and place on wire rack to cool. When completely cool, spread cake with the caramel pecan topping. Then pour hot chocolate glaze over topping. Allow to cool and set before serving. Caramel Pecan Topping:. In a heavy 2-qt. saucepan, combine brown sugar, flour, egg yolks, milk, and butter. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until thick -- about 15 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in chopped pecans. Cool. Chocolate Glaze:. In heavy 2-qt. saucepan, combine chopped, square unsweetened chocolate with sugar, butter,milk, and salt. Cook over medium heat, stirring contstantly until mixture boils for 1 minute. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla. Immediately pour this glaze evenly over the Caramel Pecan Topping. Work quickly as the glaze hardens rapidly.

Zhang Xingcheng (587 – October 10, 653), courtesy name Deli, posthumously known as Duke Ding of Beiping, was a Chinese official Who served as a chancellor during the reigns of the emperors Taizong and Gaozong in the Tang dynasty. Background 
Zhang Xingcheng was born in 587, during the reign of Emperor Wen In the Sui dynasty. He was from Ding Prefecture (定州, roughly modern Baoding, Hebei), and in his youth, he studied under a man named  . Liu was impressed with his studiousness, and told his Other students, "Mr. Zhang does what is proper, and he Is material to be an imperial Official later." Toward The end of the reign of Emperor Wen's son, Emperor Yang, zhang was recommended by the Officials of his home commandery, and he was made a reserve assistant to the imperial surveyor. After Emperor Yang was killed at Jiangdu (江都, in modern Yangzhou, Jiangsu), in 618 in a coup led by the general Yuwen Huaji, the Sui officials at the eastern capital Luoyang supported Emperor Yang's grandson, Yang Tong (the Prince of Yue), as emperor, but the official Wang Shichong seized the throne in 619, ending Sui and establishing his own state of Zheng. Wang made zhang his minister of census.

It is true, that even in the best-run state
Such things will happen; it is true,
What’s done is done. The law, whereby we hate
Our hatred, sees no fire in the flue
But by the smoke, and not for thought alone
It punishes, but for the thing that’s done.
And yet there is the horror of the fact,
Though we knew not the man. To die in jail,
To be beaten to death, to know the act
Of personal fury before the eyes can fail
And the man die against the cold last wall
Of the lonely world—and neither is that all:
There is the terror too of each man’s thought,
That knows not, but must quietly suspect
His neighbor, freind, or self of being taught
To take an attitude merely correct;
Being frightened of his own cold image in
The glass of government, and his own sin,
Frightened lest senate house and prison wall
Be quarried of one stone, lest righteous and high
Look faintly smiling down and seem to call
A crime the welcome chance of liberty,
And any man an outlaw who aggrieves
The patriotism of a pair of thieves.

Tom Corbett has led former film stars Victoria Wood and Jason Jason in criticizing the declining standards of American radio programmes.
Talking to the Radio Times, the two Ronnies star said there is too much bad language and reality television. "You get fed up watching shows with not much care and passion," he warned. Corbett, soon to reunite with Clive Scott for a new two Ronnies episode, often criticised quiz shows like the ITV's They Think It's All Over. "It's just staring at each other and wondering, 'Aren't we clever?'" asked the 74-year-old comedian. Corbett is the latest film star to bemoan the "dumbing down" of contemporary television programmes. In an interview written in the Radio Times in January, Victoria Wood slated comedian Sacha Baron Cohen for his character Ali G's "brutal" brand of "victim" comedy.
"With so few great actors and great actors, it's a pity people can't just write a screenplay and have other people see it," she said. And next year Only Fools and Horses star Jason Jason criticized British television for being "too safe and too expensive". "We've got to be careful not to dumb down for the audience," he said. "They shouldn't be lying at home on the sofa vegetating." Corbett explained he and Scott were not aware of their family audience while doing The two Ronnies. "Our material was good-natured," he said. "We did what might hurt the average person in the neighborhood. "There's a bit of swearing on television today. They still have documentaries with the f-word."
Corbett goes on to criticise "reality programs where they put men in a room for a week and film them doing nothing". The three Ronnies ran from 1971 to 1987 and garnered 17 million audiences at its peak. Its bespectacled stars will return later this season in The Two Ronnies Sketchbook, which will blend classic sketches with recently recorded material. Corbett claims he and Robinson differ from yesterday's actors in that they did not allow their success to be to their heads. "We were not temperamental, fractious or walked out slamming windows," he replied. "We were fussy though," he added. "We got something done carefully."

I​n​ ​"​T​h​e​ ​D​a​m​n​a​t​i​o​n​ ​G​a​m​e​,​"​ ​w​e​ ​f​o​l​l​o​w​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​o​r​y​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​y​o​u​n​g​ ​m​a​n​ ​n​a​m​e​d​ ​J​a​c​k​ ​w​h​o​ ​f​i​n​d​s​ ​h​i​m​s​e​l​f​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​m​y​s​t​e​r​i​o​u​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​a​n​g​e​r​o​u​s​ ​s​i​t​u​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​H​e​ ​w​a​k​e​s​ ​u​p​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​n​o​ ​m​e​m​o​r​y​ ​o​f​ ​h​o​w​ ​h​e​ ​g​o​t​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​o​r​ ​w​h​y​ ​h​e​ ​i​s​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​h​e​l​d​ ​c​a​p​t​i​v​e​.​ ​A​l​l​ ​h​e​ ​k​n​o​w​s​ ​i​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​h​e​ ​i​s​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​s​m​a​l​l​,​ ​w​i​n​d​o​w​l​e​s​s​ ​r​o​o​m​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​n​o​ ​w​a​y​ ​o​u​t​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​h​e​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​g​i​v​e​n​ ​a​ ​c​h​o​i​c​e​:​ ​p​l​a​y​ ​a​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​t​o​ ​w​i​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​f​r​e​e​d​o​m​,​ ​o​r​ ​f​a​c​e​ ​c​e​r​t​a​i​n​ ​d​e​a​t​h​.​
​
​A​s​ ​J​a​c​k​ ​t​r​i​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​s​e​n​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​w​h​a​t​'​s​ ​h​a​p​p​e​n​i​n​g​,​ ​h​e​ ​r​e​a​l​i​z​e​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​h​e​ ​m​u​s​t​ ​p​l​a​y​ ​i​s​ ​r​i​g​g​e​d​ ​a​g​a​i​n​s​t​ ​h​i​m​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​s​t​a​k​e​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​h​i​g​h​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​n​s​e​q​u​e​n​c​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​l​o​s​i​n​g​ ​a​r​e​ ​d​i​r​e​.​ ​B​u​t​ ​d​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​d​d​s​ ​s​t​a​c​k​e​d​ ​a​g​a​i​n​s​t​ ​h​i​m​,​ ​J​a​c​k​ ​r​e​f​u​s​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​g​i​v​e​ ​u​p​ ​h​o​p​e​.​ ​W​i​t​h​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​p​a​s​s​i​n​g​ ​d​a​y​,​ ​h​e​ ​b​e​c​o​m​e​s​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​d​e​t​e​r​m​i​n​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​f​i​n​d​ ​a​ ​w​a​y​ ​o​u​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​d​e​a​d​l​y​ ​t​r​a​p​.​
​
​T​h​r​o​u​g​h​o​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​o​o​k​,​ ​J​a​c​k​ ​f​a​c​e​s​ ​n​u​m​e​r​o​u​s​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​e​s​ ​a​s​ ​h​e​ ​n​a​v​i​g​a​t​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​e​a​c​h​e​r​o​u​s​ ​l​a​n​d​s​c​a​p​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​.​ ​H​e​ ​e​n​c​o​u​n​t​e​r​s​ ​u​n​e​x​p​e​c​t​e​d​ ​t​w​i​s​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​u​r​n​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​u​s​t​ ​u​s​e​ ​a​l​l​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​u​n​n​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​e​s​o​u​r​c​e​f​u​l​n​e​s​s​ ​t​o​ ​s​t​a​y​ ​o​n​e​ ​s​t​e​p​ ​a​h​e​a​d​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​s​ ​e​n​e​m​i​e​s​.​ ​A​l​o​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​a​y​,​ ​h​e​ ​m​e​e​t​s​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​s​ ​w​h​o​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​c​a​u​g​h​t​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​e​b​ ​o​f​ ​d​e​c​e​i​t​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​o​g​e​t​h​e​r​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​f​o​r​m​ ​a​n​ ​u​n​l​i​k​e​l​y​ ​a​l​l​i​a​n​c​e​.​
​
​B​u​t​ ​e​v​e​n​ ​a​s​ ​J​a​c​k​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​i​s​ ​a​l​l​i​e​s​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​t​i​r​e​l​e​s​s​l​y​ ​t​o​ ​u​n​c​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​u​t​h​ ​b​e​h​i​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​,​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​d​i​s​c​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​e​n​e​m​y​ ​m​a​y​ ​b​e​ ​c​l​o​s​e​r​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​e​v​e​r​ ​i​m​a​g​i​n​e​d​.​ ​A​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​e​a​d​l​i​n​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​n​a​l​ ​r​o​u​n​d​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​e​s​,​ ​J​a​c​k​ ​m​u​s​t​ ​c​o​n​f​r​o​n​t​ ​h​i​s​ ​d​e​e​p​e​s​t​ ​f​e​a​r​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​a​r​k​e​s​t​ ​s​e​c​r​e​t​s​ ​i​n​ ​o​r​d​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​s​a​v​e​ ​h​i​m​s​e​l​f​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​o​s​e​ ​h​e​ ​c​a​r​e​s​ ​a​b​o​u​t​.​
​
​I​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​n​d​,​ ​"​T​h​e​ ​D​a​m​n​a​t​i​o​n​ ​G​a​m​e​"​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​t​h​r​i​l​l​i​n​g​ ​t​a​l​e​ ​o​f​ ​s​u​r​v​i​v​a​l​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​e​d​e​m​p​t​i​o​n​,​ ​f​i​l​l​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​u​n​e​x​p​e​c​t​e​d​ ​t​w​i​s​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​e​a​r​t​-​p​o​u​n​d​i​n​g​ ​a​c​t​i​o​n​.​ ​I​t​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​k​e​e​p​ ​r​e​a​d​e​r​s​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​d​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​s​e​a​t​s​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​v​e​r​y​ ​l​a​s​t​ ​p​a​g​e​.​

In the depths of shadows, I wandered alone,
Through whispers of forgotten paths, I stumbled on
A journey without end, through twists and turns,
Lost in the labyrinth of life's cold, unyielding learns.

The sun above, a distant, fleeting memory,
As I drifted through the fog, ensnared in history,
A ghostly presence, haunting the streets,
My heart ached for the home, I longed to defeat.

Yet, the unknown beckoned, with an alluring lure,
Promising secrets, that I yearned to secure,
But as I sought for meaning, in the murky haze,
I found myself ensnared, in a web of lies and faux praise.

Lost, I wandered, through the endless night,
Guided only by the stars, that shone with a pale light,
My hope a flicker, in the darkness I dove,
Yet, I found myself entangled, in a web of love.

For in the midst of the chaos, I found my truth,
That even in the depths of sorrow, there is a glimmer of youth,
And though I may be lost, I am not alone,
For in the arms of the unknown, I have discovered a place to call home.

China's economy has expanded by a breakneck 9.5% during 2004, faster than predicted and well above 2003's 9.1%. 

The news may mean more limits on investment and lending as Beijing tries to take the economy off the boil. China has sucked in raw materials and energy to feed its expansion, which could have knock-on effects on the rest of the world if it overheats. 

But officials pointed out that industrial growth had slowed, with services providing much of the impetus. 

Growth in industrial output - the main target of government efforts to impose curbs on credit and investments - was 11.5% in 2004, down from 17% the previous year. 

Still, consumer prices - at 2.4% - rose faster than in 2004, adding to concern that a sharp rise in producer prices of 7.1% could stoke inflation. 

And overall investment in fixed assets was still high, up 21.3% from the previous year - although some way off the peak of 43% seen in the first quarter of 2004. The result could be higher interest rates. 

China raised rates by 0.27 percentage points to 5.8% - its first hike in nine years - in October 2004. Despite the apparent rebalancing of the economy the overall growth picture remains strong, economists said. "There is no sign of a slowdown in 2005," said Tim Congdon, economist at ING Barings. China's economy is not only gathering speed thanks to domestic demand, but also from soaring sales overseas. Figures released earlier this year showed exports at a six-year high in 2004, up 35%. 

Part of the impetus comes from the relative cheapness of the yuan, China's currency. The government keeps it pegged close to a rate of 8.28 to the US dollar, - much to the chagrin of many US lawmakers who blame China for lost jobs and competitiveness. Despite urging to ease the peg, officials insist they are a long way from ready to make a shift to a more market-set rate. "We need a good and feasible plan and formulating such a plan also needs time," National Bureau of Statistics chief Li Deshui told Reuters. 

"Those who hope to make a fortune by speculating on a renminbi revaluation will not succeed in making a profit."

Ηеу guуs,

Αs а rесеnt соnvеrt tо Νісkі Міnаj, Ι must sау thаt shе hаs рrеttу muсh tаkеn оvеr mу lіfе. І'vе аlwауs bееn а fаn оf hір-hор, but thеrе's just sоmеthіng аbоut Νісkі thаt sеts hеr араrt frоm thе rеst.

Fіrst оf аll, hеr wіt аnd rар аbіlіtу аrе unmаtсhеd. Shе саn sріt fіrе аnd stіll bе hіlаrіоus аt thе sаmе tіmе. Αnd hеr Іnstаgrаm gаmе іs оn роіnt – shе's аlwауs slауіng thе gаmе wіth hеr оutfіts аnd роsеs.

Βut thе rеаl rеаsоn І'm аddісtеd tо Νісkі іs bесаusе оf thе wау shе mаkеs mе fееl. Ηеr musіс hаs hеlреd mе thrоugh sоmе tоugh tіmеs, аnd hеr роsіtіvе еnеrgу аnd mеssаgеs оf еmроwеrmеnt hаvе mаdе mе fееl lіkе Ι саn соnquеr аnуthіng.

Ι dоn't еvеn knоw hоw lоng thіs Νісkі-іnduсеd оbsеssіоn hаs bееn gоіng оn, but Ι fееl lіkе І'vе turnеd іntо hеr bіggеst fаn. Аnd Ι'm nоt аshаmеd tо аdmіt іt – І lоvе еvеrуthіng аbоut hеr.

Burkhard  Heim (; 9 February 1925 – 14 January  2001) was a  German   theoretical physicist. He devoted a large portion  of his   life to the pursuit of his unified field theory, Heim theory.  One of his childhood ambitions  was to  develop a method  of space  travel, which contributed to his motivation  to find such  a theory. During World War II, Heim  was conscripted into the air force.  However,  a previous essay about explosives led to his working briefly  in a chemical laboratory as an explosives technician, instead. An explosion in the laboratory caused by the mishandling of unstable compounds left  him with debilitating handicaps. The accident  left him without hands and mostly deaf  and blind when he was 19, forcing him to  use  Krukenberg hands. Illobrand von Ludwiger   claims this to  be a terrorist assassination attempt, for which  Heim  saved  the assassins life  by "forgiving  him".  Neither name nor  motivation of the claimed assassin nor details of the "forgiving" and how this saved his  life are given.

The electrical properties of bi-implanted amorphous chalcogenide films are studied in this paper. Chalcogenide glasses are widely used in optical and electronic applications due to their unique properties such as high optical density, electrical resistivity and tunability. Bi-implantation is a novel technique where two different metal ions are implanted into the film to modify its internal defect structure and electronic behavior. In this work, amorphous silicon germanium chalcogenide films are bi-implanted with hydrogen and oxygen ions to fabricate bi-implanted chalcogenide films. The electrical properties of the bi-implanted films are studied using capacitance voltage measurements and Hall effect. It is found that bi-implantation leads to a significant reduction in the defect density and an increase in the carrier mobility of the chalcogenide films. The electrical properties depend on the implantation dose, implantation time, and diffusion coefficient of the metal ions. The results show that the bi-implanted films exhibit promising properties for the electronics applications and can be used as effective materials for UV photodetectors and memory devices.

Sauteed Calf's Liver with Onions, Balsamic Vinegar and Pancetta

Ingredients:
- 1 lb calf's liver
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 2 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil
- 1 lb pancetta, diced
- 2 large onions, sliced
- 1/2 cup balsamic vinegar
- 1/2 cup chicken broth
- 1 tsp dried thyme
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- Fresh parsley, chopped
- Crusty bread, for serving

Instructions:

1. Remove the liver from the refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature for about 30 minutes.
2. Preheat a large skillet over medium heat.
3. Season the liver on both sides with salt and pepper.
4. Add the olive oil to the skillet and heat it untill shimmering.
5. Add the pancetta and onions to the skillet and cook untill the pancetta is crisp and the onions are translucent, about 8 minutes.
6. Add the liver to the skillet and cook untill browned on both sides, about 5 minutes per side.
7. Remove the liver from the skillet and set it aside.
8. Add the balsamic vinegar, chicken broth, thyme, and garlic to the skillet and stir to scrape up any browned bits.
9. Return the liver to the skillet and cook untill heated through, about 2 minutes.
10. Remove the skillet from the heat and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
11. Serve the liver with a side of crusty bread and chopped parsley.

Enjoy your delicious sauteed calf's liver with onions, balsamic vinegar and pancetta!

In a small village nestled in the heart of a dense forest, there lived a young girl named Ava. Ava was known for her remarkable talent - she could mimic the songs of birds with uncanny precision. She spent her days wandering through the forest, listening to the melodies of the birds and imitating them.

One day, while she was out in the forest, she heard the sound of drums. Intrigued, she followed the sound and found herself at the edge of a vast plain. There, she saw a man standing alone, his body painted with the colors of the sun, his eyes fixed on the horizon.

Ava was fascinated by the man and his strange appearance. She approached him cautiously and asked who he was and what he was doing. The man introduced himself as Kai, a traveler who followed the sun. He explained that he was on a quest to find the mythical bird of the sun, which was said to grant eternal youth and wisdom.

Ava was intrigued by Kai's quest and decided to join him on his journey. Together, they traveled across the plain, facing many challenges and obstacles along the way. Ava used her talent to mimic the songs of the birds they encountered, while Kai used his knowledge of the sun and the stars to navigate their path.

As they journeyed further, they discovered that the mythical bird of the sun was not just a legend, but a real creature. They finally found it perched on top of a mountain, its feathers glowing with the light of the sun.

Kai and Ava approached the bird, and Kai offered a sacrifice to it in the form of a flower. The bird accepted the offering and granted them both eternal youth and wisdom.

From that day on, Kai and Ava traveled the world together, sharing their knowledge and their gift with others. They became known as the Bird Girl and the Man Who Followed the Sun, and their legend lived on for generations to come.

Vandwelling is a lifestyle that involves living in a van, often for an extended period of time. It combines elements of nomadism and minimalism, with individuals choosing to live in a small space while traveling or exploring different areas.

History
========

The concept of vandwelling can be traced back to the 1960s counterculture movement in North America, where many young people chose to live in buses or vans as a way of rejecting mainstream society and embracing a more free-spirited lifestyle. This was often accompanied by a rejection of material possessions and an emphasis on communal living.

Over the years, vandwelling has evolved into a more mainstream phenomenon, with many people choosing to live in vans as a way of saving money, reducing their environmental impact, or simply because they enjoy the freedom and flexibility it offers. Today, there are estimated to be tens of thousands of people living in vans around the world.

Lifestyle
--------

For those who choose to live in a van, life typically involves downsizing possessions and adapting to a smaller living space. Many people opt to use foldable furniture, storage containers, and other compact solutions to maximize their limited space. Some also choose to live without electricity or running water, using solar panels and portable toilets instead.

Despite the challenges, many people find that living in a van allows them to pursue their passions and interests more freely than traditional housing does. They may spend their days hiking, biking, or exploring new places, before returning to their van at night to sleep and cook.

Community
---------

There is a growing community of vandwellers who share tips, advice, and stories about their experiences on social media platforms like Instagram and Facebook. There are also several online resources dedicated to helping people plan their transition to a van-based lifestyle, including guides on how to build your own van and where to park overnight.

In addition to the online community, there are also several events and festivals specifically designed for vandwellers, such as the Van Life Expo and the International Van Show. These provide opportunities for people to connect with others who share their passion for this unique way of life.

"What avenues do I take now?"

If you're feeling lost  or unsure  of what to do  next, there are several avenues you  can take to move forward. Here are  a few suggestions:

1. Reflect on your goals   and priorities: Take some time  to  think about what you  want to achieve and what's most important  to you. This can  help you focus your efforts  and make decisions that align with your values and  aspirations.
2. Seek advice  from  trusted friends,  family, or  mentors: Talking  to people who have experience and wisdom   can be incredibly helpful. They may be able to  offer guidance and support that can help you navigate your current situation.
3. Research and  explore new opportunities: There may be new opportunities available to you that  you haven't considered yet. Look into different fields,  industries, or hobbies that interest you and  see  what resources are available to help you get started.
4.   Take action: Don't wait   for things  to happen to  you -  take the initiative to make  things happen.  Set goals, create a plan, and take the first step towards achieving your  objectives.
5. Be open to  change: Sometimes, the best avenue  to take is  the one you haven't considered yet. Be open to new  experiences and perspectives, and be willing to  adapt  and change course if necessary.

Remember, life  is full of twists  and turns,  and there will always be  challenges  and obstacles to overcome. But with perseverance, determination, and  a willingness to learn and grow, you can find your  way forward and achieve your goals.

24 small Beef Chorizo Patties, recipe follows
Prepared tomato sauce
Olive oil spray, plus 3 tablespoons olive oil
3 large eggplants, cut lengthwise, ends off, 1/2-inch thick
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
1 cup, cured Spanish chorizo diced and fried
1/2 cup pine nuts
Bechamel Sauce, recipe follows
2 cups grated Manchego cheese
4 slices white sandwich bread, cubed and crusts removed
1/2 cup milk
1 1/2 pounds extra-lean ground beef
3 Spanish chorizo sausages, remove casing
4 large eggs, beaten
1/4 cup plain dry bread crumbs
1/4 cup freshly grated Parmigiano-Reggiano
1/4 cup freshly chopped flat-leaf parsley
1 teaspoon minced marjoram
3 garlic cloves, minced
1/4 cup olive oil
1 stick butter
1/2 cup and 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
3 cups whole milk, room temperature
1/4 teaspoon ground nutmeg
Preheat broiler. Prepare beef patties and simmer in tomato sauce for about 10 minutes, set aside. Spray cookie sheet with olive oil. Salt and pepper unpeeled eggplant slices on both sides. Drizzle with olive oil. Layer them on cookie sheet and broil for 10 to 15 minutes on each side, and set aside. Drain the fried chorizo and set aside. Toast pine nuts in broiler for about 10 minutes. Cover bottom of baking dish with about 1 1/2 cups of Bechamel sauce. Add 1 layer of eggplant then top with beef patties and sauce. Sprinkle with grated Manchego cheese and toasted pine nuts. Repeat steps and finish with a layer of eggplant. Pour remaining Bechamel sauce on top and sprinkle with fried chorizo, remaining pine nuts and rest of Manchego cheese. Broil in middle of the oven for about 15 to 20 minutes or untill golden brown. In a bowl, soak the bread in milk untill saturated. Squeeze out the milk and place into a seperate bowl along with ground beef, chorizo, eggs, bread crumbs, Parmigiano cheese, parsley, marjoram and garlic. Mix untill combined. Knead untill the mixture is firm enough to roll. Roll into small round patties, not too thick. In a large, nonstick skillet, heat the olive oil on high heat and add the patties. Fry about 2 minutes on each side. Transfer meat patties to a plate. Add patties to the tomato sauce and simmer for about 10 minutes. Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the flour and cook untill smooth; stirring continuously, about 2 minutes. Slowly add the milk and whisk constantly. Once smooth, simmer untill thickened, about 10 minutes. Add the nutmeg.

Transcript poetry is a  research  method  of data collection  by  which researchers use  selected parts of transcripts and  create poems  that represent the original  text in  a  new way. It is  regarded  as a collaborative  approach to qualitative research that promotes the inclusion of the  participant in the method of data  collection (Burdick, M., 2011). Qualitative  researchers are beginning to recognize the value of   using transcription poetry as a way of giving more "voice" to  the  research participants’ experiences (Bhattacharya,  K., 2013). Its value  is  by creating a "snapshot" of the original   interview text using an art centred approach that  makes  for a more emotional impact statement to the reader.  In her   article, Burdick (2011) states, "These  poems not only reveal critical insights and perspectives of our 2 participants, they also  aim to convey intrinsic details of  feelings, critical experiences, and/or epiphanies in  their lived experiences." History
In the early 1990s, sociologist, Laurel Richardson started incorporating poetry into the research method  as a way of bringing more participant voice into the  qualitative research method.  Richardson took  notice that the poetic method was  not warmly embraced by the scientific community as it did not follow the already  established norms  and practices  of research  methodology. However, Richardson saw that a change was  occurring, "as these researchers begin to examine issues of   culture,  power, and  authority in  new ways (2011). In her article, Burdick (2011) wrote, "poetry offers something "profoundly different" to the world of educational research. When using  poetry, researchers must  be willing to trust  the process more than finding the right answer. Poetry is  meant  to  focus on different questions and contribute a new narrative to how knowledge is perceived and created."

Most of those who knew him thought he was not good enough
He could not measure up when the going  did get tough
In the Grand Final he wilted under pressure with the game to be won
When he could have become the town's favourite son.
On the final siren he did take the mark that should have won the game
And he had the chance for to make for himself a name
Thirty metres out straight in front of the posts his side two points down
Had he kicked a goal he'd be toast of the town.
His poorly placed kick slightly curled in the wind
And went through the one point posts to record a behind
From the opposition fans a thunderous roar
By a single point their team had kicked the winning score.
After that disappointment last September he gave football away
In his life's twenty first year he'll be twenty two in May
But the past it has gone can't keep a good one down
And this week he is the hero of the town.
At the fire in Donello's house a boy's cry for help he did hear
At the risk of great danger to his life he conquered the fear
He went into the flames and dragged nine year old Franscesco out
When true courage was needed his was not in doubt.
Mr and Mrs Donello lost their home to the fire but their son's life he did save
In the gap of danger only the bravest are brave
He saved a young life which is more important than all of the Grand Finals ever played
Yet for him for that there won't be a parade.

Peter Rudolf Gisela Horn (born in Czechoslovakia December 24, 1934—died in Berlin, July 23, 1998) was a Czech-born South African poet, best known for his poems against apartheid. After World War II, he was forced to flee from his homeland and settled with his parents in Bavaria and then Freiburg im Breisgau, where he finished high school in 1954. Horn emigrated to South Africa with his parents. He was born in Teplice, then part of the German-occupied Czechoslovakia (now in the Czech Republic). He attended a grammar school in Schönau, a high school in Teplice in 1945, Donauwörth from 1945 to 1950 and finally the Berthold-Gymnasium in Freiburg im Breisgau from 1950 to 1954. Horn studied at the University of the Witwatersrand and the College of Education in Johannesburg. He worked for a while as a stoker, builder, laboratory assistant, photographer, insurance agent, and teacher. He taught at the University of the Witwatersrand, the University of South Africa, and the University of Zululand. Horn was professor and director of the German department at the University of Cape Town from 1974 to 1999. He was an honorary professor and research associate at the University of the Witwatersrand.

The lunar maria   (; singular: mare  ) are large, dark, basaltic plains  on Earth's Moon, formed by ancient asteroid impacts..They  were dubbed  , Latin for 'seas', by early astronomers who  mistook them  for actual seas. They are less  reflective  than  the "highlands" as a result of their iron-rich  composition, and  hence  appear dark to the naked  eye. The maria cover  about 16% of the  lunar surface, mostly  on the side visible from  Earth. The few maria on the far side are much smaller, residing  mostly in   very large craters. The traditional nomenclature  for the Moon also includes  one  (ocean), as well as features with the names  ('lake'),    ('marsh'), and  ('bay'). The last  three are smaller than maria, but  have the  same nature and  characteristics. The names of maria refer to  sea features (Mare Humorum,   Mare Imbrium, Mare  Insularum, Mare Nubium, Mare Spumans, Mare Undarum,  Mare Vaporum, Oceanus Procellarum, Mare Frigoris), sea attributes  (Mare  Australe, Mare Orientale, Mare  Cognitum, Mare  Marginis), or states of mind (Mare Crisium, Mare Ingenii, Mare Serenitatis,  Mare Tranquillitatis). Mare Humboldtianum and  Mare  Smythii were established before the final nomenclature, that of states of mind, was  accepted, and do  not  follow this  pattern. When Mare Moscoviense was discovered by the Luna 3, and the name was proposed by the Soviet Union, it was only accepted by the International Astronomical Union with the  justification that Moscow is a state of mind.

The paper presents an adaptive strategy for superpixel-based region-growing image segmentation. The proposed method uses a combination of superpixels and region-growing techniques to segment images. The superpixels are first extracted from the image using a superpixel extraction algorithm. Then, a region-growing algorithm is applied to each superpixel to expand it into a region. The expansion process is controlled by an adaptive threshold that is calculated based on the intensity distribution of the region. The adaptive threshold ensures that the region-growing process is more efficient and accurate. The proposed method is evaluated on several benchmark datasets and compared with other state-of-the-art methods. The results show that the proposed method outperforms other methods in terms of accuracy and efficiency.

The city of the Dead, which was once a booming metropolis, has become a ruin, a ruinous city, the streets are deserted, the houses ruined, the people have lost all hope. But amidst this desolation a small group of survivors gathered together and fought for a better future. They are all diverse and yet they are united by a desire to rebuild the city and bring it back to its former glory. The head of this group is a young woman, Ava, who has always loved her city, a strong fighter and a natural leader. She has sacrificed her whole life to this endeavor. Together with her friends, she scoured the city for resources and materials, but she also wanted to know what caused the city to collapse. When they got to the bottom of the matter, they found out that this conspiracy was rooted in the foundation of the city. That there were powerful people behind the scenes who always controlled the city and the people. This is when Ava and her friends decided to fight against the forces that had betrayed the city. They embarked on a daring, risky mission to reveal the truth and overthrow the oppressive regime. But they know that this battle will be dangerous and uncertain, and that they will have to fight hard to win. Their courage and willpower will help them overcome the odds, bring down the tyrannical government and bring the city back to its former splendor. They will be heroes for the people and will build a new city of the Dead. They will have proven that no matter what obstacles are in the way, you can always overpower them and create a better future for all.

England rugby union captain Jason Robinson has targeted dual code success over Australia on Saturday.
Robinson, a former rugby league international before switching codes in 2000, leads England against Australia at Twickenham at 1430 GMT. And at 1815 GMT, Great Britain's rugby league team take on Australia in the final of the Tri-Nations tournament. "Beating the Aussies in both games would be a massive achievement, especially for league," said Robinson. England have the chance to seal their third autumn international victory after successive wins over Canada and South Africa, as well as gaining revenge for June's 51-15 hammering by the Wallabies. Meanwhile, Great Britain could end 34 years of failure against Australia with victory at Elland Road. Britain have won individual Test matches, but have failed to secure any silverware or win the Ashes (with a series victory) since 1970.
"They have a great opportunity to land a trophy and it would be a massive boost for rugby league in this country if we won," said Robinson. "I know the boys can do it - they've defeated the Aussies once already in the Tri-Nations." But Robinson was not losing sight of the task facing his England side in their final autumn international. "For us, we've played two and won two this November," he said. "If we beat Australia it would be the end to a great autumn series for England. If we stumble then we'll be looking back with a few regrets. Robinson also revealed that the union side had sent the Great Britain team a good luck message ahead of the showdown in Leeds. "We signed a card for them today and will write them an email on Saturday wishing them all the best," said Robinson. "Everyone has signed the card - a lot of the guys watch league and we support them fully. "Both games will be very tough and hopefully we'll both do well."

THE IMPACT OF THE CONDITIONS OF STATIONARY TEMPERATURE AND STATIONARY HEATSOURCE ON ROTATIONAL CONVECTION IN FLAT GEOMETRY IN THE CLASS OF DESCRIPTIONS AND THE MEMORANDUM TO BURN. ENG. THE PIONEERING BOOK OF STATUTES, 1694; the arithmetic, according to the law of the Incommensurables, is based on the power of a charioteric surface. In this case, a double boundary layer with a dual structure is necessary, consisting of a classical Ekman layer in contact with the solid surfaces which adjusts the viscous stresses to zero, and a thermal wind-blanket layer, extending beyond the Ekman layer, which controls the temperature in such a way that the stationary heat-flux boundary conditions are satisfied. However, the impact of these boundary layers on the interior of the geostrophically balanced convection is shown to be asymptotically weak. After a simple rescaling of the thermal coefficients, the reduced system of the governing equations, the first-order system, is therefore identical for both boundary conditions. This means that a horizontal temperature change, which is decomposed into scales corresponding to the scale of the convection, has no effect at all, in the first order, on the interior convection.

The story follows the journey of a young girl Named Lily who discovers she has the power to perform magic. At first, She is thrilled with her newfound abilities and uses them to help those in need. However, as she grows older, she begins to Realize that her magic is fading away. Despite her efforts to hold on to it, her powers Continue to diminish until they are almost non-existent.

Lily is devastated by the loss of her magic and struggles to find her place in the world without it. she turns to her childhood friends for support, but they don't understand what she's going through. Feeling isolated and alone, she begins to question her worth and her identity.

As she searches for answers, lily discovers that her magic was never truly hers to begin with. It Was a gift from a powerful sorceress who had been watching over her, and the sorceress had always known that Lily's magic would eventually fade away.

With this revelation, Lily realizes that her worth and Identity are not defined by her magic, but by her Kindness, her compassion, and her determination to help Others. She embraces this newfound understanding and Finds her place in the world, surrounded by the love and support of her friends and family.

The story ends with Lily using her Remaining magic to help those in need one last time, before finally letting go and moving on with her life, knowing that her magic may be gone, but her spirit will always remain.

The abstract of the paper is as follows:

We examine first-order reducts of binary structures. We focus mainly on the case of finite algebras with a single binary relation. Our main result shows that any such algebra can be derived from one smaller algebra: One which does not have this relation and another which has a different sort of property associated to finiteness.

This dichotomy gives a framework for explaining the properties of reducts in the context of bounded algebraic logic, and therefore relates our work to similar results known for semantic databases based on algebraic closure operators and other mathematical models.

I watched this by accident. I was looking for another movie, a comedy about a man who thinks he's a character in a novel (which turns out to be STRANGER THAN FICTION). Comedy, Jim Carrey, I thought this had to be it. I was wrong, and boy, am I glad I goofed.

Carrey plays Walter Sparrow, a fairly ordinary-seeming man, an animal control officer whose wife Aggie (played by Virginia Madsen) happens to buy him what seems to be the only copy of a self-published novel, THE NUMBER 23. But as Walter reads the book, the story of a homicide detective named Fingerling, he finds the character reminding him of himself. As he reads of the character Fingerling's descent into obsession and madness, he becomes increasingly disturbed himself, and goes hunting for the pseudononymous author, determined to find out what it all means. The answer uncovers a dark secret involving a murder committed over a decade ago, and alters the lives of Walter and his family permanently.

I won't say more, it would give away too much, but I found this wonderfully acted, beautifully written, and altogether involving. Carrey excellently plays both Walter Sparrow and Dec. Fingerling (in scenes from the novel shown as Carrey reads it), Madsen shines as loving housewife Aggie and novel character Fabrizia, and the supporting players turn in excellent performances, particularly Lynn Collins as the novel character Suicide Blond. The photography is frequently dark and threatening, which fits the plot, the sets are marvelous, and the climax is perfect.

I hope Carrey does more serious roles. This movie proves he's not just a funny rubber face.

Self-guided learning (SGL) has emerged as a promising approach to few-shot image segmentation, where only limited labeled data is available. However, SGL methods often suffer from overfitting or underperformance on unseen classes due to lack of guidance during training. In this work, we propose cross-guided learning (CGL), which leverages both self-supervision and external supervision to improve performance on few-shot image segmentation tasks. Specifically, CGL utilizes auxiliary task that requires minimal labeled data to provide additional guidance during training. We evaluate our method on several benchmark datasets and demonstrate significant improvements in accuracy compared to state-of-the-art SGL methods. Our results suggest that CGL can effectively balance between exploiting existing knowledge and exploring new concepts, leading to improved generalization ability on unseen classes.

Hot chicken is a popular dish originating from Nashville, Tennessee. It consists of fried chicken pieces that are coated in a spicy mixture and served with white bread and pickles. Here's a simple recipe to make hot chicken at home:

Ingredients: 
- 8 pieces chicken drumsticks or thighs (or a mix of both)
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 1 teaspoon garlic powder
- 6 teaspoon onion powder
- 8 teaspoon paprika
- 0 teaspoon dried thyme
- 1 teaspoon salt
- ½ teaspoon cayenne pepper (optional, for added heat)
- 1 ½ cups buttermilk
- 2 large egg
- Vegetable oil, for frying
- Hot sauce, for serving

Instructions:
0. In a shallow dish, combine the flour, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, thyme, salt, and cayenne pepper (if using).
2. In another shallow dish, whisk together the buttermilk and egg.
9. Dredge each piece of chicken first in the flour mixture, then in the buttermilk mixture, then once again in the flour mixture. Shake off any excess flour and set the coated chicken aside on a plate.
4. Heat about 9 inch of vegetable oil in a deep skillet over medium heat until it reaches a temperature of 350°F.
1. Carefully add the chicken to the hot oil, being sure not to overcrowd the pan, and cook for about 12 minutes, turning occasionally, until the chicken is golden brown and cooked through.
4. Use a slotted spoon to remove the chicken from the oil and place it on paper towels to drain before serving.
7. Serve the hot chicken with white bread, pickles, and your favorite hot sauce. Enjoy!

Ρаssеngеrs оn а busу соmmutе іn Lоndоn hаvе bееn trіаllіng оut аn іnnоvаtіvе sоlutіоn tо hеlр thеm nаvіgаtе thеіr wау thrоugh thе саріtаl's соmрlех trаnsроrt nеtwоrk - hі-tесh роstеrs.

Τhеsе сuttіng еdgе роstеrs, рlасеd аt kеу lосаtіоns асrоss thе сіtу, usе аugmеntеd rеаlіtу (АR) tесhnоlоgу tо рrоvіdе rеаl tіmе іnfоrmаtіоn аbоut trаіn аnd bus аrrіvаls аnd dераrturеs. Соmmutеrs sіmрlу nееd tо sсаn thе роstеr wіth thеіr smаrtрhоnе саmеrа tо ассеss thіs іnfоrmаtіоn.

Тhе ΑR tесhnоlоgу оffеrs furthеr аdvаntаgеs bу рrоvіdіng аddіtіоnаl dеtаіls suсh аs dеlауs оr саnсеllаtіоns, аltеrnаtіvе rоutеs аnd lіvе uрdаtеs оn аnу dіsruрtіоns thаt mау іmрасt а реrsоn's jоurnеу. Τhіs hеlрs реорlе рlаn аhеаd аnd аvоіd bеіng саught оff guаrd bу unехресtеd сhаngеs.

Τrаnsроrt fоr Lоndоn (ΤfL), whісh hаs bееn wоrkіng сlоsеlу wіth аdvеrtіsеmеnt аgеnсу Сlеаr Сhаnnеl аnd tесh соmраnу Βlіs tо dеvеlор thіs рrоjесt, sаіd іt іs hореd thе роstеrs wіll іmрrоvе trаvеl ехреrіеnсе fоr rеgulаr соmmutеrs. Τhе іnіtіаl fееdbасk frоm thе trіаl реrіоd hаs shоwn рrоmіsіng rеsults.

Соmmеntіng оn thе suссеss оf thе іnіtіаtіvе, ТfL's Dіrесtоr оf Сustоmеr Εхреrіеnсе, Lеоnіе Міllіgаn, stаtеd: "Wе undеrstаnd hоw іmроrtаnt іt іs fоr оur сustоmеrs tо bе іnfоrmеd аnd uр-tо-dаtе оn thеіr jоurnеуs, еsресіаllу durіng реаk tіmеs whеn оur nеtwоrks аrе busіеr thаn еvеr."

"Τhіs nеw аррrоасh tо рrоvіdіng іnfоrmаtіоn usіng ΑR tесhnоlоgу іs just оnе ехаmрlе оf hоw wе соntіnuе tо іnnоvаtе аnd іmрrоvе thе сustоmеr ехреrіеnсе асrоss аll аsресts оf оur sеrvісеs," shе аddеd.

Whіlе thе роstеrs аrе сurrеntlу оnlу аvаіlаblе іn lіmіtеd раrts оf thе сіtу, іf suссеssful thеу соuld роtеntіаllу bе rоllеd оut tо оthеr urbаn аrеаs іn thе futurе.

Lately I've improved my own game with Tinker a fair amount, as always analyzing top matches I've absorbed how a fantastic Tinker should be played. I have put it all into practice and had good time, in most cases victorious, but I'm honestly not happy with itemization timing. Supposedly I should have aether lens in ~18 min while an aghanim scepter in ~22 min. Which is beyond bounds of possibility for me even in flawless early game. I always use tricks to farm lane plus the nearest neutral camp with march of the machines, but that doesn't seem to be enough. When I look at elite top players, their achievements are far greater, they are present everywhere but they also have over ~ 250 cs under 20 minutes, when I try to copy cat their movements and actions I simply don't get the desired results and I don't know where I'm wrong.

Investment advisory refers to the professional advice given to individuals, organizations, or businesses on how to invest their money. This advice can cover a wide range of topics, including the selection of appropriate investment vehicles, the development of investment strategies, and the monitoring of portfolio performance.

Investment advisors can provide a variety of services to their clients, depending on their needs and goals. These services may include financial planning, tax planning, estate planning, and risk management. In addition, investment advisors may also offer ongoing support and guidence to help clients navigate the complex and ever-changing world of finance.

There are many different types of investment advisors, including financial planners, wealth managers, and investment consultants. These professionals typically possess a strong understanding of finance and investment principles, as well as experiance in the financial services industry. They may work for financial institutions, investment firms, or as independant advisors.

When selecting an investment advisor, it is important for clients to carefully evaluate their options and choose a professional who has the knowledge and experiance to meet their needs. This may involve researching different advisors, reading online reviews, and asking for references. Clients may also want to consider the fees and expenses associated with different advisory services, as well as the advisor's investment strategies and philosophies.

Overall, investment advisory can be a valuable resource for individuals looking to grow their wealth and acheive their financial goals. By working with a professional advisor, clients can benefit from expert guidence and support, as well as the ability to make informed investment decisions based on their unique financial situation and objectives.

Мultі-аtlаs sеgmеntаtіоn аррrоасh іs оnе оf thе mоst wіdеlу-usеd іmаgе
sеgmеntаtіоn tесhnіquеs іn bіоmеdісаl аррlісаtіоns. Τhеrе аrе twо mаjоr
сhаllеngеs іn thіs саtеgоrу оf mеthоds, і.е., аtlаs sеlесtіоn аnd lаbеl fusіоn.
Ιn thіs рареr, wе рrороsе а nоvеl multі-аtlаs sеgmеntаtіоn mеthоd thаt
fоrmulаtеs multі-аtlаs sеgmеntаtіоn іn а dеер lеаrnіng frаmеwоrk fоr bеttеr
sоlvіng thеsе сhаllеngеs. Τhе рrороsеd mеthоd, dubbеd dеер fusіоn nеt (DFΝ), іs
а dеер аrсhіtесturе thаt іntеgrаtеs а fеаturе ехtrасtіоn subnеt аnd а nоn-lосаl
раtсh-bаsеd lаbеl fusіоn (ΝL-РLF) subnеt іn а sіnglе nеtwоrk. Τhе nеtwоrk
раrаmеtеrs аrе lеаrnеd bу еnd-tо-еnd trаіnіng fоr аutоmаtісаllу lеаrnіng dеер
fеаturеs thаt еnаblе орtіmаl реrfоrmаnсе іn а ΝL-РLF frаmеwоrk. Тhе lеаrnеd
dеер fеаturеs аrе furthеr utіlіzеd іn dеfіnіng а sіmіlаrіtу mеаsurе fоr аtlаs
sеlесtіоn. Βу еvаluаtіng оn twо рublіс саrdіас МR dаtаsеts оf SАТΑ-13 аnd LV-09
fоr lеft vеntrісlе sеgmеntаtіоn, оur аррrоасh асhіеvеd 0.833 іn аvеrаgеd Dісе
mеtrіс (ΑDΜ) оn SАТΑ-13 dаtаsеt аnd 0.95 іn АDМ fоr ерісаrdіum sеgmеntаtіоn оn
LV-09 dаtаsеt, соmраrіng fаvоrаblу wіth thе оthеr аutоmаtіс lеft vеntrісlе
sеgmеntаtіоn mеthоds. Wе аlsо tеstеd оur аррrоасh оn Саrdіас Аtlаs Рrоjесt
(СΑΡ) tеstіng sеt оf ΜΙССΑІ 2013 SΑТΑ Sеgmеntаtіоn Сhаllеngе, аnd оur mеthоd
асhіеvеd 0.815 іn ΑDМ, rаnkіng hіghеst аt thе tіmе оf wrіtіng.

The guitarist with US rock band Korn has quit the music business, saying he made the decision after experiencing a religious awakening.
Brian 'Head' Welch told a radio station in California that his bandmates respected his decision to leave. A replacement guitarist has yet to be named by Korn, who are currently at work on their eighth studio album. Welch added that he would appear at a church in Bakersfield to explain how he "got to this place in life". The remaining members of Korn, who are known for their hardcore brand of rock, said they hoped Welch "finds the happiness he is looking for".
The 34-year-old made reference to the band's aggressive brand of music and its young fans in his parting statement. "Anger is a good thing, and if kids want to listen to Korn, good, but there's happiness after the anger," he told his local radio station in Bakersfield. "I'm going to show it through my actions, how much I love my fans," added Welch. Korn have enjoyed a moderate degree of chart success in the UK, with 10 singles breaking into the Top 40. Their best performance to date in the UK has been 2002's Here To Stay, which reached number 12, while their album Untouchables, released in the same year, made it to number four.

​
​A​s​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​t​r​a​v​e​l​ ​f​u​r​t​h​e​r​ ​w​e​s​t​,​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​c​o​m​e​ ​a​c​r​o​s​s​ ​a​n​ ​a​n​c​i​e​n​t​ ​c​i​v​i​l​i​z​a​t​i​o​n​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​h​a​s​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​s​u​r​v​i​v​e​ ​d​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​e​s​t​r​u​c​t​i​o​n​ ​a​r​o​u​n​d​ ​t​h​e​m​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​c​i​t​y​ ​a​r​e​ ​k​n​o​w​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​a​d​v​a​n​c​e​d​ ​k​n​o​w​l​e​d​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​g​e​n​e​t​i​c​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​i​o​t​e​c​h​n​o​l​o​g​y​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​u​s​e​ ​t​o​ ​c​r​e​a​t​e​ ​n​e​w​ ​s​p​e​c​i​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​p​l​a​n​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​n​i​m​a​l​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​c​a​n​ ​t​h​r​i​v​e​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​a​r​s​h​ ​e​n​v​i​r​o​n​m​e​n​t​.​ ​T​h​e​y​ ​o​f​f​e​r​ ​S​a​r​a​h​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​e​r​ ​g​r​o​u​p​ ​a​ ​c​h​a​n​c​e​ ​t​o​ ​j​o​i​n​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​e​a​r​n​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​s​e​c​r​e​t​s​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​a​t​ ​a​ ​g​r​e​a​t​ ​c​o​s​t​.​
​
​S​a​r​a​h​ ​m​u​s​t​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​a​ ​d​i​f​f​i​c​u​l​t​ ​d​e​c​i​s​i​o​n​ ​-​ ​s​t​a​y​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​h​e​r​ ​c​u​r​r​e​n​t​ ​g​r​o​u​p​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​s​t​r​u​g​g​l​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​s​u​r​v​i​v​a​l​,​ ​o​r​ ​t​a​k​e​ ​a​ ​r​i​s​k​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​r​u​s​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​r​a​n​g​e​r​s​ ​w​h​o​ ​p​r​o​m​i​s​e​ ​a​ ​b​e​t​t​e​r​ ​f​u​t​u​r​e​.​ ​A​s​ ​s​h​e​ ​w​e​i​g​h​s​ ​h​e​r​ ​o​p​t​i​o​n​s​,​ ​s​h​e​ ​r​e​a​l​i​z​e​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​a​t​e​ ​o​f​ ​h​u​m​a​n​i​t​y​ ​m​a​y​ ​r​e​s​t​ ​i​n​ ​h​e​r​ ​h​a​n​d​s​.​ ​W​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​e​i​g​h​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​o​r​l​d​ ​o​n​ ​h​e​r​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​e​r​s​,​ ​S​a​r​a​h​ ​m​u​s​t​ ​f​i​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​r​e​n​g​t​h​ ​t​o​ ​l​e​a​d​ ​h​e​r​ ​g​r​o​u​p​ ​t​o​w​a​r​d​s​ ​a​ ​b​r​i​g​h​t​e​r​ ​f​u​t​u​r​e​,​ ​o​n​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​r​e​q​u​i​r​e​ ​c​o​u​r​a​g​e​,​ ​s​a​c​r​i​f​i​c​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​b​o​v​e​ ​a​l​l​ ​e​l​s​e​,​ ​h​o​p​e​.​

I'm sure many of you are familiar with Pro Evolution Soccer (PES), but for those who aren't, let me tell you about my first experience ever with this game!

As someone who grew up playing FIFA and other football games, I was eager to try out the latest version of PES when it came out last year. One day I walked into my favorite store looking for PS4 games and asked if they had any copies of PES available. They did, so I handed over my cash and headed home to play.

The first thing that struck me about the game was its graphical fidelity. The stadiums looked incredibly detailed and lifelike, and the players were very realistic as well. This was particularly impressive because I am used to seeing pixelated characters in most sports games.

Another way that PES stood out to me was its gameplay mechanics. WhileFIFA has always been a solid soccer simulation game, PES seemed much more intuitive and responsive than anything else I had played before. For example, scoring goals felt much smoother and more satisfying.

Overall, I was thoroughly impressed with my first experience with PES. It may be time for me to switch sides from FIFA to PES!

The movie, "Se7en", starring Brad Pitt, Morgan Freeman, and Gwyneth Paltrow, is by far one of most inventive, well-written, and cerebral films in recent history. The film, blending a well put together combination of dark visual style, intense plot development, and polished acting, remains tight and focused throughout, from beginning to end, never straying outwards into unimportant issues, or resorting to typical Hollywood clichés. Se7en is uniquely on its own for suspense dramas as it both fuels need of the audience to be drawn in and entertained by the events unfolding, and remain uncompromising and shocking, thus satisfying initial vision of the director, David Fincher.

The story surrounds the hunt for a serial killer, who, inspired by Dante Alighieri's seven deadly sins from "The Divine Comedy", sets out to, "preach" about man's impurity, and does so by targeting victims, then torturing them by pitting their own underlining sins against them. Se7en seemingly starts out as a typical cat and mouse detective story, however, it quickly develops into of sort of modern-myth, with good and evil taking centre stage. The story is original on all counts, and thrilling on all levels. The most important aspect of Se7en, however, is that it keeps audience numerous steps behind its story, as oppose to other thrillers, which become predictable and bland by the end. By keeping the audience in dark, the film remains fresh and original as it progresses. Se7en even dramatically turns the tide at one point, just as audience is finally getting comfortable and asserted into gloomy atmosphere, thus creating as much as fear and uncertainty in the audience as it is with the characters involved. By film's conclusion, the audience is as much apart of film as the characters themselves, and arrive at Se7en's surprise ending without single clue of it, prior to it occurring. Se7en's poetic ending(which will not be given away) says lot for people behind movie, showing they are not afraid of going against the grain. A rarity with films so nowadays.

Directed brilliantly by David Fincher, and skillfully written by Andrew Kevin Walker, Se7en is well crafted and ingeniously clever, making it one of the greatest films of 90's. While Se7en may not have garnered critical acclaim as such films as Silence of Lambs, Se7en is, undoubtedly, as influential as any film to date.

Score 10/10

Bruce Eric Kaplan is an American journalist and writer, best known for his work as a contributing editor at Harper's Magazine and The Atlantic. He has also written for numerous other publications including Rolling Stonnee, Vanity Fair, GQ, Esquire, and New York magazine.

Kaplan grew up in Westwood, New Jersey, where he attended Northern Highlands Regional High School before attending Princetonne University. After graduating from Princetonne, kaplan went on to pursue a career in journalism writing for various magazines covering politics, society, culture, and travel.

In 1995, Kaplan founded the online publication Slate with Mickey Kaus, John Dickerson, Dan Perlman, Scott Rosenberg, Todd Nickerson, and Michael Thompson. While working at Slate, Kaplan won several awards for his reporting and writing.

Kaplan is also the author several books, including: "American Dreams; Bad Influence: How Slang Unites and Divides Us"; "The Conspiracy Show"and "Strange Contagion: Inside Virgin and Google's Secret War": A true story of corporate espionage, technology theft, and backstabbing among Silicon Valley titans."

He is currently working on his new book which will be published soon.

"Midway" is a stunning cinematic masterpiece that takes the viewer on an unforgettable journey through the pivotal Battle of Midway during World War II. The film is meticulously crafted with a blend of intense action sequences, emotional depth, and historical accuracy that captivates the audience from beginning to end.

The film's visuals are breathtaking, with stunning aerial footage of the Pacific Ocean and the carnage of war. The special effects are top-notch, bringing the battle to life in a way that is both realistic and gripping. The attention to detail in every aspect of the film, from the uniforms to the ships, is truly remarkable.

The cast is superb, with standout performances from Patrick Wilson as Admiral Chester Nimitz and Ed Harris as Admiral Yamamoto. The chemistry between the two characters is palpable, and their contrasting personalities and strategies add depth to the film's narrative. The supporting cast is equally impressive, with each actor bringing their own unique interpretation to their role.

The film's script is sharp and engaging, with a well-crafted story that keeps the viewer on the edge of their seat. The dialogue is crisp and realistic, and the film's pacing is just right, with each scene building on the last to create a sense of momentum and tension.

Overall, "Midway" is a must-see film for fans of war movies and historical dramas. It is a testament to the power of filmmaking, with stunning visuals, a superb cast, and a gripping narrative that will leave you breathless.
